17	
18	from app.agents.workspace.paths import USER_UPLOADED_DIRNAME, safe_upload_filename
19	from app.db.chroma.chromadb import ChromaClient
20	from app.db.mongodb.collections import files_collection
21	from app.db.utils import serialize_document
22	from app.decorators.caching import CacheInvalidator
23	from app.models.files_models import DocumentSummaryModel
24	from app.models.message_models import FileData as MessageFileData
25	from app.services.artifact_events import publish_artifact_event, upload_event
26	from app.services.storage import (
27	    FsOps,
28	    JuiceFSUnavailable,
29	    chmod_path,
30	    fs_timer,
31	    session_root,
32	    write_session_file,
33	)
34	from app.utils.file_utils import generate_file_summary
35	from app.utils.upload_validation import validate_upload
36	from shared.py.wide_events import log
37	
38	
39	@CacheInvalidator(
40	    key_patterns=[
41	        "files:{user_id}:*",
42	    ]
43	)
44	async def upload_file_service(
45	    file: UploadFile,
46	    user_id: str,
47	    conversation_id: str | None = None,
48	    content_length: int | None = None,
49	) -> dict:
50	    """Upload a file to Cloudinary, generate embeddings, and store metadata in MongoDB and ChromaDB."""
51	    content, normalized_content_type, resource_type = await validate_upload(
52	        file=file, content_length=content_length
53	    )
54	
55	    file_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
56	    # validate_upload() guarantees filename is present — narrow the type here
57	    # without asserting (bandit B101).
58	    filename = file.filename or ""
59	    public_id = f"file_{file_id}_{filename.replace(' ', '_')}"
60	    log.set(
61	        service="file_service",
62	        operation="upload",
63	        user_id=user_id,
64	        filename=filename,
65	        content_type=normalized_content_type,
66	        file_id=file_id,
67	    )
68	
69	    try:
70	        file_size = len(content)
71	
72	        cloudinary_task = asyncio.to_thread(
73	            cloudinary.uploader.upload,
74	            io.BytesIO(content),
75	            resource_type=resource_type,
76	            public_id=public_id,
77	            overwrite=True,
78	        )
79	
80	        summary_task = generate_file_summary(
81	            file_content=content,
82	            content_type=normalized_content_type,
83	            filename=filename,
84	        )
85	
86	        upload_result, summary_result = await asyncio.gather(
87	            cloudinary_task,
88	            summary_task,
89	        )
90	
91	        file_url = upload_result.get("secure_url")
92	        if not file_url:
93	            log.error("Missing secure_url in Cloudinary upload response")
94	            raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail="Invalid response from file upload service")
95	
96	        summary, formatted_file_content = _process_file_summary(summary_result)
97	
98	        sandbox_path: str | None = None
99	        if conversation_id:
100	            try:
101	                safe_filename = safe_upload_filename(filename)
102	            except ValueError as e:
103	                log.warning(f"[upload] skipping sandbox copy: {e}")
104	            else:
105	                sandbox_path = await _persist_upload_to_sandbox(
106	                    user_id=user_id,
107	                    conversation_id=conversation_id,
108	                    safe_filename=safe_filename,
109	                    content=content,
110	                    content_type=normalized_content_type,
111	                )
112	                await _persist_summary_to_sandbox(
113	                    user_id=user_id,
114	                    conversation_id=conversation_id,
115	                    safe_filename=safe_filename,
116	                    summary_md=_render_summary_markdown(
117	                        filename=filename,
118	                        content_type=normalized_content_type,
119	                        description=summary,
120	                        page_wise_summary=formatted_file_content,
121	                    ),
122	                )
123	
124	        current_time = datetime.now(UTC)
125	        file_metadata = {
126	            "file_id": file_id,
127	            "filename": filename,
128	            "type": normalized_content_type,
129	            "size": file_size,
130	            "url": file_url,
131	            "public_id": public_id,
132	            "user_id": user_id,
133	            "description": summary,
134	            "page_wise_summary": formatted_file_content,
135	            "sandbox_path": sandbox_path,
136	            "created_at": current_time,
137	            "updated_at": current_time,
138	        }
139	        if conversation_id:
140	            file_metadata["conversation_id"] = conversation_id
141	
142	        # Store in DB (Mongo + Chroma)
143	        await asyncio.gather(
144	            _store_in_mongodb(file_metadata),
145	            _store_in_chromadb(
146	                file_id=file_id,
147	                user_id=user_id,
148	                filename=filename,
149	                content_type=normalized_content_type,
150	                conversation_id=conversation_id,
151	                file_description=summary_result,
152	            ),
153	        )
154	
155	        return {
156	            "file_id": file_id,
157	            "url": file_url,
158	            "filename": filename,
159	            "description": summary,
160	            "type": normalized_content_type,
161	            "sandbox_path": sandbox_path,
162	        }
163	
164	    except HTTPException:
165	        raise
166	    except Exception as e:
167	        log.error(f"Failed to upload file: {e!s}", exc_info=True)
168	        raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail=f"Failed to upload file: {e!s}")
169	
170	
171	async def _persist_upload_to_sandbox(
172	    user_id: str,
173	    conversation_id: str,
174	    safe_filename: str,
175	    content: bytes,
176	    content_type: str,
177	) -> str | None:
178	    """Mirror an upload into the session's read-only `user-uploaded/` dir.
179	
180	    Returns the `/workspace/...` path the agent can operate on, or None if
181	    JuiceFS is unavailable (dev). Cloudinary remains the durable copy; a
182	    failure here must never fail the upload.
183	    """
184	    try:
185	        async with fs_timer(FsOps.UPLOAD_PERSIST_SANDBOX):
186	            # Clear a prior read-only copy so re-uploads (last-writer-wins) work.
187	            prior = session_root(user_id, conversation_id) / USER_UPLOADED_DIRNAME / safe_filename
188	            with contextlib.suppress(Exception):
189	                if prior.exists():
190	                    await chmod_path(prior, 0o644)
191	
192	            host_path, sbx_path = await write_session_file(
193	                user_id=user_id,
194	                conversation_id=conversation_id,
195	                relative_path=f"{USER_UPLOADED_DIRNAME}/{safe_filename}",
196	                content=content,
197	            )
198	            await chmod_path(host_path, 0o444)
199	    except JuiceFSUnavailable as e:
200	        log.warning("[upload] juicefs unavailable; sandbox_path not set", error=str(e))
201	        return None
202	    except Exception as e:
203	        log.error("[upload] juicefs write failed", error=str(e), exc_info=True)
204	        return None
205	
206	    # Cross-mount host writes may not reach the sandbox watcher; publish the
207	    # artifact event directly so the chat UI sees the upload immediately.
208	    await publish_artifact_event(
209	        user_id,
210	        upload_event(
211	            conversation_id,
212	            safe_filename,
213	            size_bytes=len(content),
214	            content_type=content_type,
215	        ),
216	    )
217	    return sbx_path
218	
219	
220	def _render_summary_markdown(
221	    filename: str,
222	    content_type: str,
223	    description: str | None,
224	    page_wise_summary: list[dict[str, Any]] | dict[str, Any] | None,
225	) -> str:
226	    """Render a file's stored summary into the markdown sidecar body.
227	
228	    Pure projection of MongoDB data: the short description plus, when present,
229	    the per-page content/summaries. No recompute.
230	    """
231	    parts: list[str] = [f"# Summary: {filename}", "", f"- Type: `{content_type}`", ""]
232	    if description:
233	        parts += ["## Overview", "", description.strip(), ""]
234	
235	    if isinstance(page_wise_summary, list):
236	        for page in page_wise_summary:
237	            data = page.get("data", {})
238	            parts += [f"## Page {data.get('page_number', '?')}", ""]
239	            if page.get("summary"):
240	                parts += [f"**Summary:** {page['summary'].strip()}", ""]
241	            if data.get("content"):
242	                parts += [str(data["content"]).strip(), ""]
243	    elif isinstance(page_wise_summary, dict):
244	        content = page_wise_summary.get("data", {}).get("content")
245	        if content:
246	            parts += ["## Content", "", str(content).strip(), ""]
247	
248	    return "\n".join(parts).rstrip() + "\n"
249	
250	
251	async def _persist_summary_to_sandbox(
252	    user_id: str,
253	    conversation_id: str,
254	    safe_filename: str,
255	    summary_md: str,
256	) -> None:
257	    """Write the file's full summary to a `<file>.summary.md` sidecar in the session.
258	
259	    Best-effort, mirroring `_persist_upload_to_sandbox`: a JuiceFS failure (dev)
260	    or any write error must never fail the upload — the inline summary in the
261	    agent context comes from MongoDB independently.
262	    """
263	    relative_path = f"{USER_UPLOADED_DIRNAME}/{safe_filename}.summary.md"
264	    try:
265	        prior = session_root(user_id, conversation_id) / relative_path
266	        with contextlib.suppress(Exception):
267	            if prior.exists():
268	                await chmod_path(prior, 0o644)
269	
270	        host_path, _ = await write_session_file(
271	            user_id=user_id,
272	            conversation_id=conversation_id,
273	            relative_path=relative_path,
274	            content=summary_md.encode("utf-8"),
275	        )
276	        await chmod_path(host_path, 0o444)
277	    except JuiceFSUnavailable:
278	        return
279	    except Exception as e:
280	        log.warning("[upload] summary sidecar write failed", error=str(e))
281	
282	
283	def _process_file_summary(
284	    file_summary: str | list[DocumentSummaryModel] | DocumentSummaryModel,
285	) -> tuple:
286	    """Helper function to process file description into the correct format."""
287	    if isinstance(file_summary, str):
288	        return file_summary, None
289	    if isinstance(file_summary, list):
290	        content_str = ""
291	        content_model: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
292	        for x in file_summary:
293	            content_model.append(x.model_dump(mode="json"))
294	            content_str += x.summary
295	        return content_str, content_model
296	    if isinstance(file_summary, DocumentSummaryModel):
297	        content_str = file_summary.summary
298	        content_model_dict = file_summary.model_dump(mode="json")
299	        return content_str, content_model_dict
300	    log.error("Invalid file description format")
301	    raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="Invalid file description format")
302	
303	
304	async def _store_in_mongodb(file_metadata: dict) -> None:
305	    """Helper function to store file metadata in MongoDB."""
306	    result = await files_collection.insert_one(document=file_metadata)
307	    if not result.inserted_id:
308	        raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail="Failed to store file metadata")
309	
310	
311	async def _store_in_chromadb(
312	    file_id: str,
313	    user_id: str,
314	    filename: str,
315	    content_type: str,
316	    file_description,
317	    conversation_id: str | None = None,
318	) -> None:
319	    """Helper function to store file data in ChromaDB."""
320	    try:
321	        chroma_documents_collection = await ChromaClient.get_langchain_client(
322	            collection_name="documents"
323	        )
324	
325	        documents = []
326	        ids = []
327	
328	        if isinstance(file_description, list):
329	            for page in file_description:
330	                metadata = {
331	                    "file_id": file_id,
332	                    "user_id": user_id,
333	                    "filename": filename,
334	                    "type": content_type,
335	                    "page_number": page.data.page_number,
336	                }
337	
338	                if conversation_id:
339	                    metadata["conversation_id"] = conversation_id
340	
341	                documents.append(
342	                    Document(
343	                        page_content=page.summary,
344	                        metadata=metadata,
345	                    )
346	                )
347	                ids.append(str(uuid.uuid4()))  # Generate a new ID for each page
348	        else:
349	            metadata = {
350	                "file_id": file_id,
351	                "user_id": user_id,
352	                "filename": filename,
353	                "type": content_type,
354	            }
355	
356	            if conversation_id:
357	                metadata["conversation_id"] = conversation_id
358	
359	            documents.append(
360	                Document(
361	                    page_content=(
362	                        file_description
363	                        if isinstance(file_description, str)
364	                        else file_description.summary
365	                    ),
366	                    metadata=metadata,
367	                )
368	            )
369	            ids.append(file_id)
370	
371	        # Store document metadata in ChromaDB
372	        await chroma_documents_collection.aadd_documents(
373	            ids=ids,
374	            documents=documents,
375	        )
376	        log.info(f"File with id {file_id} indexed in ChromaDB")
377	    except Exception as chroma_err:
378	        # Log but don't fail if ChromaDB indexing fails
379	        log.error(
380	            f"Failed to index file in ChromaDB: {chroma_err!s}",
381	            exc_info=True,
382	        )
383	
384	
385	async def update_file_in_chromadb(
386	    file_id: str,
387	    user_id: str,
388	    filename: str,
389	    content_type: str,
390	    file_description,
391	    conversation_id: str | None = None,
392	) -> None:
393	    """Helper function to update file data in ChromaDB."""
394	    try:
395	        # First try to delete the existing records
396	        try:
397	            chroma_documents_collection = await ChromaClient.get_langchain_client(
398	                collection_name="documents"
399	            )
400	            await chroma_documents_collection.adelete(ids=[file_id])
401	            log.info(f"Removed old file data for {file_id} from ChromaDB")
402	        except Exception as delete_err:
403	            # Just log and continue with the new insertion
404	            log.warning(f"Could not delete old file data from ChromaDB: {delete_err!s}")
405	
406	        # Now store the updated file data
407	        await _store_in_chromadb(
408	            file_id=file_id,
409	            user_id=user_id,
410	            filename=filename,
411	            content_type=content_type,
412	            file_description=file_description,
413	            conversation_id=conversation_id,
414	        )
415	
416	    except Exception as chroma_err:
417	        # Log but don't fail if ChromaDB update fails
418	        log.error(
419	            f"Failed to update file in ChromaDB: {chroma_err!s}",
420	            exc_info=True,
421	        )
422	
423	
424	@CacheInvalidator(
425	    key_patterns=[
426	        "files:{user_id}:*",
427	    ]
428	)
429	async def delete_file_service(file_id: str, user_id: str | None) -> dict:
430	    """Delete a file by ID for the user, removing it from MongoDB, Cloudinary, and ChromaDB."""
431	    log.info(f"Deleting file with id: {file_id} for user: {user_id}")
432	    log.set(service="file_service", operation="delete", file_id=file_id, user_id=user_id)
433	
434	    if user_id is None:
435	        log.error("User ID is required to delete a file")
436	        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="User ID is required")
437	
438	    # Retrieve file metadata before deletion
439	    file_data = await files_collection.find_one({"file_id": file_id, "user_id": user_id})
440	    if not file_data:
441	        log.error(f"File with id {file_id} not found for user {user_id}")
442	        raise HTTPException(
443	            status_code=404, detail="File not found"
444	        )  # Get the conversation_id for cache invalidation
445	
446	    public_id = file_data.get("public_id")
447	    if not public_id:
448	        log.warning(f"File {file_id} has no public_id for Cloudinary deletion")
449	
450	    # Delete from MongoDB
451	    result = await files_collection.delete_one({"file_id": file_id, "user_id": user_id})
452	    if result.deleted_count == 0:
453	        log.error("File not found for deletion in MongoDB")
454	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="File not found")
455	
456	    # Delete from Cloudinary if public_id exists
457	    if public_id:
458	        try:
459	            cloudinary_result = cloudinary.uploader.destroy(public_id)
460	            if cloudinary_result.get("result") != "ok":
461	                log.warning(f"Failed to delete file from Cloudinary: {cloudinary_result}")
462	        except Exception as e:
463	            # Log but don't fail if Cloudinary deletion fails
464	            log.error(f"Error deleting file from Cloudinary: {e!s}", exc_info=True)
465	
466	    try:
467	        chroma_documents_collection = await ChromaClient.get_langchain_client(
468	            collection_name="documents"
469	        )
470	        await chroma_documents_collection.adelete(ids=[file_id])
471	        log.info(f"File with id {file_id} deleted from ChromaDB")
472	    except Exception as e:
473	        # Log the error but don't fail the request if ChromaDB deletion fails
474	        log.error(f"Failed to delete file from ChromaDB: {e!s}")
475	
476	    # Cache invalidation is handled by the CacheInvalidator decorator
477	    log.info(f"File {file_id} successfully deleted")
478	
479	    return {
480	        "message": "File deleted successfully",
481	        "file_id": file_id,
482	        "filename": file_data.get("filename", "Unknown"),
483	    }
484	
485	
486	@CacheInvalidator(
487	    key_patterns=[
488	        "files:{user_id}:*",
489	    ]
490	)
491	async def update_file_service(
492	    file_id: str,
493	    user_id: str,
494	    update_data: dict,
495	    file_content: bytes | None = None,
496	    conversation_id: str | None = None,
497	) -> dict:
498	    """Update file metadata, regenerating the description from new content if provided.
499	
500	    Refreshes the ChromaDB embedding and invalidates related caches.
501	    """
502	    log.info(f"Updating file with id: {file_id} for user: {user_id}")
503	    log.set(service="file_service", operation="update", file_id=file_id, user_id=user_id)
504	
505	    file_data = await files_collection.find_one({"file_id": file_id, "user_id": user_id})
506	    if not file_data:
507	        log.error(f"File with id {file_id} not found for user {user_id}")
508	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="File not found")
509	
510	    # Store original conversation ID if not provided in update
511	    if not conversation_id:
512	        conversation_id = file_data.get("conversation_id")
513	
514	    # Prepare update data
515	    current_time = datetime.now(UTC)
516	    update_data["updated_at"] = current_time
517	
518	    # Generate new description if file content is provided
519	    if file_content:
520	        try:
521	            # Use the same file description generator as upload_file_service
522	            content_type = update_data.get("type") or file_data.get("type")
523	            filename = update_data.get("filename") or file_data.get("filename")
524	
525	            file_description = await generate_file_summary(
526	                file_content=file_content,
527	                content_type=content_type,
528	                filename=filename,
529	            )
530	
531	            # Process file description
532	            summary, page_wise_summary = _process_file_summary(file_description)
533	
534	            update_data["description"] = summary
535	            update_data["page_wise_summary"] = page_wise_summary
536	        except Exception as e:
537	            log.error(f"Failed to generate file description: {e!s}", exc_info=True)
538	            raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail=f"Failed to process file: {e!s}")
539	
540	    # Check if description is being updated
541	    description_updated = "description" in update_data
542	
543	    # Update in MongoDB
544	    result = await files_collection.update_one(
545	        {"file_id": file_id, "user_id": user_id}, {"$set": update_data}
546	    )
547	
548	    if result.modified_count == 0:
549	        log.warning(f"No changes made to file {file_id}")
550	
551	    updated_file = await files_collection.find_one({"file_id": file_id, "user_id": user_id})
552	    if not updated_file:
553	        log.error(f"Updated file {file_id} not found")
554	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="File not found after update")
555	
556	    # If description was updated, update ChromaDB
557	    if description_updated:
558	        try:
559	            # Generate new embedding for the updated description
560	            new_description = update_data["description"]
561	
562	            # Update in ChromaDB
563	            await update_file_in_chromadb(
564	                file_id=file_id,
565	                user_id=user_id,
566	                filename=updated_file.get("filename", ""),
567	                content_type=updated_file.get("type", ""),
568	                file_description=new_description,
569	                conversation_id=conversation_id,
570	            )
571	
572	        except Exception as err:
573	            # Log but don't fail if ChromaDB update fails
574	            log.error(f"Failed to update file in ChromaDB: {err!s}", exc_info=True)
575	
576	    # Convert ObjectId to string for serialization
577	    if "_id" in updated_file:
578	        updated_file["_id"] = str(updated_file["_id"])
579	
580	    # Convert date fields to ISO format
581	    for date_field in ["created_at", "updated_at"]:
582	        if date_field in updated_file and hasattr(updated_file[date_field], "isoformat"):
583	            updated_file[date_field] = updated_file[date_field].isoformat()
584	
585	    return serialize_document(updated_file)
586	
587	
588	async def get_file_descriptions(file_ids: list[str], user_id: str) -> dict[str, str]:
589	    """Fetch the stored summary for each uploaded file in one batched query.
590	
591	    Authoritative source for the agent's file context — never trust the client
592	    request for this. Returns ``{file_id: description}`` for files that have a
593	    non-empty summary; files without one are simply omitted.
594	    """
595	    if not file_ids:
596	        return {}
597	
598	    cursor = files_collection.find(
599	        {"file_id": {"$in": file_ids}, "user_id": user_id},
600	        projection={"_id": 0, "file_id": 1, "description": 1},
601	    )
602	    docs = await cursor.to_list(length=None)
603	    return {
604	        doc["file_id"]: doc["description"]
605	        for doc in docs
606	        if doc.get("file_id") and doc.get("description")
607	    }
608	
609	
610	async def _seed_one_upload(
611	    client: httpx.AsyncClient,
612	    file: MessageFileData,
613	    user_id: str,
614	    conversation_id: str,
615	) -> None:
616	    """Download one Cloudinary-hosted file and mirror it into the session sandbox."""
617	    try:
618	        safe_name = safe_upload_filename(file.filename)
619	    except ValueError:
620	        log.warning(f"[seed_uploads] skipping {file.filename!r}: invalid filename after sanitize")
621	        return
622	
623	    try:
624	        resp = await client.get(file.url)
625	        resp.raise_for_status()
626	    except Exception as e:
627	        log.warning(f"[seed_uploads] download failed for {file.filename!r}: {e}")
628	        return
629	
630	    await _persist_upload_to_sandbox(
631	        user_id=user_id,
632	        conversation_id=conversation_id,
633	        safe_filename=safe_name,
634	        content=resp.content,
635	        content_type=file.type or "application/octet-stream",
636	    )
637	
638	    # Files uploaded before the conversation existed were indexed without a
639	    # conversation_id. Stamp it now (in Mongo) so conversation-scoped search
640	    # resolves them, and project the stored summary into the sidecar. Same
641	    # association the sandbox mirror above does — applied to metadata + summary.
642	    doc = await files_collection.find_one(
643	        {"file_id": file.fileId, "user_id": user_id},
644	        projection={"_id": 0, "description": 1, "page_wise_summary": 1, "type": 1},
645	    )
646	    if not doc:
647	        return
648	
649	    if doc.get("description") or doc.get("page_wise_summary"):
650	        await _persist_summary_to_sandbox(
651	            user_id=user_id,
652	            conversation_id=conversation_id,
653	            safe_filename=safe_name,
654	            summary_md=_render_summary_markdown(
655	                filename=file.filename,
656	                content_type=doc.get("type") or file.type or "application/octet-stream",
657	                description=doc.get("description"),
658	                page_wise_summary=doc.get("page_wise_summary"),
659	            ),
660	        )
661	
662	    await files_collection.update_one(
663	        {"file_id": file.fileId, "user_id": user_id},
664	        {"$set": {"conversation_id": conversation_id}},
665	    )
666	
667	
668	async def seed_uploads_for_new_conversation(
669	    file_data: list[MessageFileData],
670	    user_id: str,
671	    conversation_id: str,
672	) -> None:
673	    """Mirror Cloudinary-backed attachments into a freshly created session's user-uploaded/ dir.
674	
675	    Files uploaded before a conversation exists land in Cloudinary only (no
676	    conversation_id → no JuiceFS write). This is called once, right after the
677	    session dirs are created, so the agent finds them at the expected path.
678	    """
679	    if not file_data:
680	        return
681	
682	    async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=30.0) as client:
683	        await asyncio.gather(
684	            *(_seed_one_upload(client, f, user_id, conversation_id) for f in file_data)
685	        )

"""
Service module for file upload functionality with vector search capabilities.
"""

import asyncio
import contextlib
from datetime import UTC, datetime
import io
from typing import Any
import uuid

import cloudinary
import cloudinary.uploader
from fastapi import HTTPException, UploadFile
import httpx
from langchain_core.documents import Document

from app.agents.workspace.paths import USER_UPLOADED_DIRNAME, safe_upload_filename
from app.db.chroma.chromadb import ChromaClient
from app.db.mongodb.collections import files_collection
from app.db.utils import serialize_document
from app.decorators.caching import CacheInvalidator
from app.models.files_models import DocumentSummaryModel
from app.models.message_models import FileData as MessageFileData
from app.services.artifact_events import publish_artifact_event, upload_event
from app.services.storage import (
    FsOps,
    JuiceFSUnavailable,
    chmod_path,
    fs_timer,
    session_root,
    write_session_file,
)
from app.utils.file_utils import generate_file_summary
from app.utils.upload_validation import validate_upload
from shared.py.wide_events import log


@CacheInvalidator(
    key_patterns=[
        "files:{user_id}:*",
    ]
)
async def upload_file_service(
    file: UploadFile,
    user_id: str,
    conversation_id: str | None = None,
    content_length: int | None = None,
) -> dict:
    """Upload a file to Cloudinary, generate embeddings, and store metadata in MongoDB and ChromaDB."""
    content, normalized_content_type, resource_type = await validate_upload(
        file=file, content_length=content_length
    )

    file_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
    # validate_upload() guarantees filename is present — narrow the type here
    # without asserting (bandit B101).
    filename = file.filename or ""
    public_id = f"file_{file_id}_{filename.replace(' ', '_')}"
    log.set(
        service="file_service",
        operation="upload",
        user_id=user_id,
        filename=filename,
        content_type=normalized_content_type,
        file_id=file_id,
    )

    try:
        file_size = len(content)

        cloudinary_task = asyncio.to_thread(
            cloudinary.uploader.upload,
            io.BytesIO(content),
            resource_type=resource_type,
            public_id=public_id,
            overwrite=True,
        )

        summary_task = generate_file_summary(
            file_content=content,
            content_type=normalized_content_type,
            filename=filename,
        )

        upload_result, summary_result = await asyncio.gather(
            cloudinary_task,
            summary_task,
        )

        file_url = upload_result.get("secure_url")
        if not file_url:
            log.error("Missing secure_url in Cloudinary upload response")
            raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail="Invalid response from file upload service")

        summary, formatted_file_content = _process_file_summary(summary_result)

        sandbox_path: str | None = None
        if conversation_id:
            try:
                safe_filename = safe_upload_filename(filename)
            except ValueError as e:
                log.warning(f"[upload] skipping sandbox copy: {e}")
            else:
                sandbox_path = await _persist_upload_to_sandbox(
                    user_id=user_id,
                    conversation_id=conversation_id,
                    safe_filename=safe_filename,
                    content=content,
                    content_type=normalized_content_type,
                )
                await _persist_summary_to_sandbox(
                    user_id=user_id,
                    conversation_id=conversation_id,
                    safe_filename=safe_filename,
                    summary_md=_render_summary_markdown(
                        filename=filename,
                        content_type=normalized_content_type,
                        description=summary,
                        page_wise_summary=formatted_file_content,
                    ),
                )

        current_time = datetime.now(UTC)
        file_metadata = {
            "file_id": file_id,
            "filename": filename,
            "type": normalized_content_type,
            "size": file_size,
            "url": file_url,
            "public_id": public_id,
            "user_id": user_id,
            "description": summary,
            "page_wise_summary": formatted_file_content,
            "sandbox_path": sandbox_path,
            "created_at": current_time,
            "updated_at": current_time,
        }
        if conversation_id:
            file_metadata["conversation_id"] = conversation_id

        # Store in DB (Mongo + Chroma)
        await asyncio.gather(
            _store_in_mongodb(file_metadata),
            _store_in_chromadb(
                file_id=file_id,
                user_id=user_id,
                filename=filename,
                content_type=normalized_content_type,
                conversation_id=conversation_id,
                file_description=summary_result,
            ),
        )

        return {
            "file_id": file_id,
            "url": file_url,
            "filename": filename,
            "description": summary,
            "type": normalized_content_type,
            "sandbox_path": sandbox_path,
        }

    except HTTPException:
        raise
    except Exception as e:
        log.error(f"Failed to upload file: {e!s}", exc_info=True)
        raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail=f"Failed to upload file: {e!s}")


async def _persist_upload_to_sandbox(
    user_id: str,
    conversation_id: str,
    safe_filename: str,
    content: bytes,
    content_type: str,
) -> str | None:
    """Mirror an upload into the session's read-only `user-uploaded/` dir.

    Returns the `/workspace/...` path the agent can operate on, or None if
    JuiceFS is unavailable (dev). Cloudinary remains the durable copy; a
    failure here must never fail the upload.
    """
    try:
        async with fs_timer(FsOps.UPLOAD_PERSIST_SANDBOX):
            # Clear a prior read-only copy so re-uploads (last-writer-wins) work.
            prior = session_root(user_id, conversation_id) / USER_UPLOADED_DIRNAME / safe_filename
            with contextlib.suppress(Exception):
                if prior.exists():
                    await chmod_path(prior, 0o644)

            host_path, sbx_path = await write_session_file(
                user_id=user_id,
                conversation_id=conversation_id,
                relative_path=f"{USER_UPLOADED_DIRNAME}/{safe_filename}",
                content=content,
            )
            await chmod_path(host_path, 0o444)
    except JuiceFSUnavailable as e:
        log.warning("[upload] juicefs unavailable; sandbox_path not set", error=str(e))
        return None
    except Exception as e:
        log.error("[upload] juicefs write failed", error=str(e), exc_info=True)
        return None

    # Cross-mount host writes may not reach the sandbox watcher; publish the
    # artifact event directly so the chat UI sees the upload immediately.
    await publish_artifact_event(
        user_id,
        upload_event(
            conversation_id,
            safe_filename,
            size_bytes=len(content),
            content_type=content_type,
        ),
    )
    return sbx_path


def _render_summary_markdown(
    filename: str,
    content_type: str,
    description: str | None,
    page_wise_summary: list[dict[str, Any]] | dict[str, Any] | None,
) -> str:
    """Render a file's stored summary into the markdown sidecar body.

    Pure projection of MongoDB data: the short description plus, when present,
    the per-page content/summaries. No recompute.
    """
    parts: list[str] = [f"# Summary: {filename}", "", f"- Type: `{content_type}`", ""]
    if description:
        parts += ["## Overview", "", description.strip(), ""]

    if isinstance(page_wise_summary, list):
        for page in page_wise_summary:
            data = page.get("data", {})
            parts += [f"## Page {data.get('page_number', '?')}", ""]
            if page.get("summary"):
                parts += [f"**Summary:** {page['summary'].strip()}", ""]
            if data.get("content"):
                parts += [str(data["content"]).strip(), ""]
    elif isinstance(page_wise_summary, dict):
        content = page_wise_summary.get("data", {}).get("content")
        if content:
            parts += ["## Content", "", str(content).strip(), ""]

    return "\n".join(parts).rstrip() + "\n"


async def _persist_summary_to_sandbox(
    user_id: str,
    conversation_id: str,
    safe_filename: str,
    summary_md: str,
) -> None:
    """Write the file's full summary to a `<file>.summary.md` sidecar in the session.

    Best-effort, mirroring `_persist_upload_to_sandbox`: a JuiceFS failure (dev)
    or any write error must never fail the upload — the inline summary in the
    agent context comes from MongoDB independently.
    """
    relative_path = f"{USER_UPLOADED_DIRNAME}/{safe_filename}.summary.md"
    try:
        prior = session_root(user_id, conversation_id) / relative_path
        with contextlib.suppress(Exception):
            if prior.exists():
                await chmod_path(prior, 0o644)

        host_path, _ = await write_session_file(
            user_id=user_id,
            conversation_id=conversation_id,
            relative_path=relative_path,
            content=summary_md.encode("utf-8"),
        )
        await chmod_path(host_path, 0o444)
    except JuiceFSUnavailable:
        return
    except Exception as e:
        log.warning("[upload] summary sidecar write failed", error=str(e))


def _process_file_summary(
    file_summary: str | list[DocumentSummaryModel] | DocumentSummaryModel,
) -> tuple:
    """Helper function to process file description into the correct format."""
    if isinstance(file_summary, str):
        return file_summary, None
    if isinstance(file_summary, list):
        content_str = ""
        content_model: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
        for x in file_summary:
            content_model.append(x.model_dump(mode="json"))
            content_str += x.summary
        return content_str, content_model
    if isinstance(file_summary, DocumentSummaryModel):
        content_str = file_summary.summary
        content_model_dict = file_summary.model_dump(mode="json")
        return content_str, content_model_dict
    log.error("Invalid file description format")
    raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="Invalid file description format")


async def _store_in_mongodb(file_metadata: dict) -> None:
    """Helper function to store file metadata in MongoDB."""
    result = await files_collection.insert_one(document=file_metadata)
    if not result.inserted_id:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail="Failed to store file metadata")


async def _store_in_chromadb(
    file_id: str,
    user_id: str,
    filename: str,
    content_type: str,
    file_description,
    conversation_id: str | None = None,
) -> None:
    """Helper function to store file data in ChromaDB."""
    try:
        chroma_documents_collection = await ChromaClient.get_langchain_client(
            collection_name="documents"
        )

        documents = []
        ids = []

        if isinstance(file_description, list):
            for page in file_description:
                metadata = {
                    "file_id": file_id,
                    "user_id": user_id,
                    "filename": filename,
                    "type": content_type,
                    "page_number": page.data.page_number,
                }

                if conversation_id:
                    metadata["conversation_id"] = conversation_id

                documents.append(
                    Document(
                        page_content=page.summary,
                        metadata=metadata,
                    )
                )
                ids.append(str(uuid.uuid4()))  # Generate a new ID for each page
        else:
            metadata = {
                "file_id": file_id,
                "user_id": user_id,
                "filename": filename,
                "type": content_type,
            }

            if conversation_id:
                metadata["conversation_id"] = conversation_id

            documents.append(
                Document(
                    page_content=(
                        file_description
                        if isinstance(file_description, str)
                        else file_description.summary
                    ),
                    metadata=metadata,
                )
            )
            ids.append(file_id)

        # Store document metadata in ChromaDB
        await chroma_documents_collection.aadd_documents(
            ids=ids,
            documents=documents,
        )
        log.info(f"File with id {file_id} indexed in ChromaDB")
    except Exception as chroma_err:
        # Log but don't fail if ChromaDB indexing fails
        log.error(
            f"Failed to index file in ChromaDB: {chroma_err!s}",
            exc_info=True,
        )


async def update_file_in_chromadb(
    file_id: str,
    user_id: str,
    filename: str,
    content_type: str,
    file_description,
    conversation_id: str | None = None,
) -> None:
    """Helper function to update file data in ChromaDB."""
    try:
        # First try to delete the existing records
        try:
            chroma_documents_collection = await ChromaClient.get_langchain_client(
                collection_name="documents"
            )
            await chroma_documents_collection.adelete(ids=[file_id])
            log.info(f"Removed old file data for {file_id} from ChromaDB")
        except Exception as delete_err:
            # Just log and continue with the new insertion
            log.warning(f"Could not delete old file data from ChromaDB: {delete_err!s}")

        # Now store the updated file data
        await _store_in_chromadb(
            file_id=file_id,
            user_id=user_id,
            filename=filename,
            content_type=content_type,
            file_description=file_description,
            conversation_id=conversation_id,
        )

    except Exception as chroma_err:
        # Log but don't fail if ChromaDB update fails
        log.error(
            f"Failed to update file in ChromaDB: {chroma_err!s}",
            exc_info=True,
        )


@CacheInvalidator(
    key_patterns=[
        "files:{user_id}:*",
    ]
)
async def delete_file_service(file_id: str, user_id: str | None) -> dict:
    """Delete a file by ID for the user, removing it from MongoDB, Cloudinary, and ChromaDB."""
    log.info(f"Deleting file with id: {file_id} for user: {user_id}")
    log.set(service="file_service", operation="delete", file_id=file_id, user_id=user_id)

    if user_id is None:
        log.error("User ID is required to delete a file")
        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="User ID is required")

    # Retrieve file metadata before deletion
    file_data = await files_collection.find_one({"file_id": file_id, "user_id": user_id})
    if not file_data:
        log.error(f"File with id {file_id} not found for user {user_id}")
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=404, detail="File not found"
        )  # Get the conversation_id for cache invalidation

    public_id = file_data.get("public_id")
    if not public_id:
        log.warning(f"File {file_id} has no public_id for Cloudinary deletion")

    # Delete from MongoDB
    result = await files_collection.delete_one({"file_id": file_id, "user_id": user_id})
    if result.deleted_count == 0:
        log.error("File not found for deletion in MongoDB")
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="File not found")

    # Delete from Cloudinary if public_id exists
    if public_id:
        try:
            cloudinary_result = cloudinary.uploader.destroy(public_id)
            if cloudinary_result.get("result") != "ok":
                log.warning(f"Failed to delete file from Cloudinary: {cloudinary_result}")
        except Exception as e:
            # Log but don't fail if Cloudinary deletion fails
            log.error(f"Error deleting file from Cloudinary: {e!s}", exc_info=True)

    try:
        chroma_documents_collection = await ChromaClient.get_langchain_client(
            collection_name="documents"
        )
        await chroma_documents_collection.adelete(ids=[file_id])
        log.info(f"File with id {file_id} deleted from ChromaDB")
    except Exception as e:
        # Log the error but don't fail the request if ChromaDB deletion fails
        log.error(f"Failed to delete file from ChromaDB: {e!s}")

    # Cache invalidation is handled by the CacheInvalidator decorator
    log.info(f"File {file_id} successfully deleted")

    return {
        "message": "File deleted successfully",
        "file_id": file_id,
        "filename": file_data.get("filename", "Unknown"),
    }


@CacheInvalidator(
    key_patterns=[
        "files:{user_id}:*",
    ]
)
async def update_file_service(
    file_id: str,
    user_id: str,
    update_data: dict,
    file_content: bytes | None = None,
    conversation_id: str | None = None,
) -> dict:
    """Update file metadata, regenerating the description from new content if provided.

    Refreshes the ChromaDB embedding and invalidates related caches.
    """
    log.info(f"Updating file with id: {file_id} for user: {user_id}")
    log.set(service="file_service", operation="update", file_id=file_id, user_id=user_id)

    file_data = await files_collection.find_one({"file_id": file_id, "user_id": user_id})
    if not file_data:
        log.error(f"File with id {file_id} not found for user {user_id}")
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="File not found")

    # Store original conversation ID if not provided in update
    if not conversation_id:
        conversation_id = file_data.get("conversation_id")

    # Prepare update data
    current_time = datetime.now(UTC)
    update_data["updated_at"] = current_time

    # Generate new description if file content is provided
    if file_content:
        try:
            # Use the same file description generator as upload_file_service
            content_type = update_data.get("type") or file_data.get("type")
            filename = update_data.get("filename") or file_data.get("filename")

            file_description = await generate_file_summary(
                file_content=file_content,
                content_type=content_type,
                filename=filename,
            )

            # Process file description
            summary, page_wise_summary = _process_file_summary(file_description)

            update_data["description"] = summary
            update_data["page_wise_summary"] = page_wise_summary
        except Exception as e:
            log.error(f"Failed to generate file description: {e!s}", exc_info=True)
            raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail=f"Failed to process file: {e!s}")

    # Check if description is being updated
    description_updated = "description" in update_data

    # Update in MongoDB
    result = await files_collection.update_one(
        {"file_id": file_id, "user_id": user_id}, {"$set": update_data}
    )

    if result.modified_count == 0:
        log.warning(f"No changes made to file {file_id}")

    updated_file = await files_collection.find_one({"file_id": file_id, "user_id": user_id})
    if not updated_file:
        log.error(f"Updated file {file_id} not found")
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="File not found after update")

    # If description was updated, update ChromaDB
    if description_updated:
        try:
            # Generate new embedding for the updated description
            new_description = update_data["description"]

            # Update in ChromaDB
            await update_file_in_chromadb(
                file_id=file_id,
                user_id=user_id,
                filename=updated_file.get("filename", ""),
                content_type=updated_file.get("type", ""),
                file_description=new_description,
                conversation_id=conversation_id,
            )

        except Exception as err:
            # Log but don't fail if ChromaDB update fails
            log.error(f"Failed to update file in ChromaDB: {err!s}", exc_info=True)

    # Convert ObjectId to string for serialization
    if "_id" in updated_file:
        updated_file["_id"] = str(updated_file["_id"])

    # Convert date fields to ISO format
    for date_field in ["created_at", "updated_at"]:
        if date_field in updated_file and hasattr(updated_file[date_field], "isoformat"):
            updated_file[date_field] = updated_file[date_field].isoformat()

    return serialize_document(updated_file)


async def get_file_descriptions(file_ids: list[str], user_id: str) -> dict[str, str]:
    """Fetch the stored summary for each uploaded file in one batched query.

    Authoritative source for the agent's file context — never trust the client
    request for this. Returns ``{file_id: description}`` for files that have a
    non-empty summary; files without one are simply omitted.
    """
    if not file_ids:
        return {}

    cursor = files_collection.find(
        {"file_id": {"$in": file_ids}, "user_id": user_id},
        projection={"_id": 0, "file_id": 1, "description": 1},
    )
    docs = await cursor.to_list(length=None)
    return {
        doc["file_id"]: doc["description"]
        for doc in docs
        if doc.get("file_id") and doc.get("description")
    }


async def _seed_one_upload(
    client: httpx.AsyncClient,
    file: MessageFileData,
    user_id: str,
    conversation_id: str,
) -> None:
    """Download one Cloudinary-hosted file and mirror it into the session sandbox."""
    try:
        safe_name = safe_upload_filename(file.filename)
    except ValueError:
        log.warning(f"[seed_uploads] skipping {file.filename!r}: invalid filename after sanitize")
        return

    try:
        resp = await client.get(file.url)
        resp.raise_for_status()
    except Exception as e:
        log.warning(f"[seed_uploads] download failed for {file.filename!r}: {e}")
        return

    await _persist_upload_to_sandbox(
        user_id=user_id,
        conversation_id=conversation_id,
        safe_filename=safe_name,
        content=resp.content,
        content_type=file.type or "application/octet-stream",
    )

    # Files uploaded before the conversation existed were indexed without a
    # conversation_id. Stamp it now (in Mongo) so conversation-scoped search
    # resolves them, and project the stored summary into the sidecar. Same
    # association the sandbox mirror above does — applied to metadata + summary.
    doc = await files_collection.find_one(
        {"file_id": file.fileId, "user_id": user_id},
        projection={"_id": 0, "description": 1, "page_wise_summary": 1, "type": 1},
    )
    if not doc:
        return

    if doc.get("description") or doc.get("page_wise_summary"):
        await _persist_summary_to_sandbox(
            user_id=user_id,
            conversation_id=conversation_id,
            safe_filename=safe_name,
            summary_md=_render_summary_markdown(
                filename=file.filename,
                content_type=doc.get("type") or file.type or "application/octet-stream",
                description=doc.get("description"),
                page_wise_summary=doc.get("page_wise_summary"),
            ),
        )

    await files_collection.update_one(
        {"file_id": file.fileId, "user_id": user_id},
        {"$set": {"conversation_id": conversation_id}},
    )


async def seed_uploads_for_new_conversation(
    file_data: list[MessageFileData],
    user_id: str,
    conversation_id: str,
) -> None:
    """Mirror Cloudinary-backed attachments into a freshly created session's user-uploaded/ dir.

    Files uploaded before a conversation exists land in Cloudinary only (no
    conversation_id → no JuiceFS write). This is called once, right after the
    session dirs are created, so the agent finds them at the expected path.
    """
    if not file_data:
        return

    async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=30.0) as client:
        await asyncio.gather(
            *(_seed_one_upload(client, f, user_id, conversation_id) for f in file_data)
        )

class ImageContext(TypedDict, total=False):
    """Image generation/analysis operation context."""

    operation: str  # "generate"|"analyze"|"generate_stream"
    prompt_length: int
    file_name: str
    mime_type: str


class BotContext(TypedDict, total=False):
    """Chat-bot platform operation context."""

    platform: str  # "discord"|"slack"|"telegram"
    operation: str


class WideEventFields(TypedDict, total=False):
    """Canonical schema for wide event fields set via log.set().

    Using consistent field names ensures LogQL queries work uniformly
    across all endpoints. Example:
        log.set(user=UserContext(id=user_id))
        log.set(chat=ChatContext(conversation_id=conv_id, message_count=5))
        log.set(model=ModelContext(name="gpt-4", provider="openai"))
    """

    user: UserContext
    chat: ChatContext
    model: ModelContext
    conversation: ConversationContext
    todo: TodoContext
    memory: MemoryContext
    calendar: CalendarContext
    goal: GoalContext
    reminder: ReminderContext
    workflow: WorkflowContext
    search: SearchContext
    payment: PaymentContext
    onboarding: OnboardingContext
    integration: IntegrationContext
    image: ImageContext
    bot: BotContext
    # Top-level convenience fields used across endpoints
    operation: str
    outcome: str

content.content:
# libs/shared

Shared utilities consumed by all GAIA Python apps (API, voice-agent, bots) and TypeScript apps (bots, CLI).

## Structure

```
py/                  - gaia-shared Python package
  logging.py         - Loguru-based logging (auto-configured on import)
  wide_events.py     - Wide event logger (one structured event per request)
  secrets.py         - Infisical secrets injection
  settings/
    base.py          - BaseAppSettings, CommonSettings (Pydantic)
    validator.py     - SettingsValidator for grouped missing-key warnings
  __init__.py        - Re-exports: get_contextual_logger, log, wide_task

ts/                  - @gaia/shared TypeScript package
  src/
    bots/            - Bot adapter, commands, GaiaClient, streaming utilities
    cli/             - CLI command manifest (descriptions shared with packages/cli)
  package.json       - private, ESM, no build step (imported directly from src/)
```

## Key Commands

```bash
# Lint Python
nx lint shared-python          # runs: uvx ruff check .

# Format Python
nx format shared-python        # runs: uvx ruff format .

# TypeScript has no separate build — imported directly via path alias
```

## How Shared Works in the Monorepo

### Python (`libs/shared/py/` → `gaia-shared`)

The Python shared package is a local `uv` workspace package named `gaia-shared`. Apps (`apps/api`, `apps/voice-agent`, `apps/bots`) declare it as a workspace dependency in their `pyproject.toml`:

```toml
dependencies = ["gaia-shared"]
```

`uv` resolves it via the workspace `[tool.uv.workspace]` config at the repo root. After adding or modifying `libs/shared/py/`, run `nx run api:sync` (or the relevant app's sync target) to refresh the lockfile.

**When to add Python code here:**
- Logging, secrets, settings — anything two or more Python apps need
- Do not add app-specific business logic; keep this package lean and generic

### TypeScript (`libs/shared/ts/` → `@gaia/shared`)

The TypeScript package is a private workspace package. It has **no build step** — apps import directly from source via the `@gaia/shared` path alias resolved by the Nx workspace. No compilation is needed after editing.

**When to add TypeScript code here:**
- Bot adapters, CLI manifest, streaming utilities, or any logic shared across `apps/bots`, `packages/cli`, or future consumers
- React/RN hooks and utilities shared between `web`, `desktop`, and `mobile`

### DRY Enforcement

If you find duplicated logic across apps, consolidate it here. Update all import sites. Do not leave dead copies behind.

## Gotchas

- **Console logging is configured on import** — just importing `shared.py.logging` activates loguru. Apps that need file logging must call `configure_file_logging(log_dir)` explicitly (the API does this; do not add it to shared itself).
- **`LOG_FORMAT=json`** must be set in Docker environments so Promtail can parse NDJSON. Console mode (`LOG_FORMAT=console`) is the default for local development.
- **Wide events use `ContextVar`** — each async task/request gets its own isolated event. HTTP middleware calls `log.reset()` at request start; ARQ workers must use the `wide_task()` context manager instead.
- **Custom log levels**: `PERFORMANCE` (3), `AUDIT` (28), `SECURITY` (38). Use `logger.bind(performance=True)` to route entries to the performance log file.
- **Infisical is not required for local dev** — missing Infisical env vars log a warning and return in non-production. In production (`ENV=production`) they raise `InfisicalConfigError`.
- **Local env vars take precedence** over Infisical secrets — Infisical only injects keys that are not already set in `os.environ`.
- **TypeScript package has no build step** — `main` and `types` both point to `src/index.ts`. It is consumed directly from source via workspace resolution.
- No inline imports. All Python code must have full type annotations enforced by mypy + ruff.

content.thinking:
I'm ready to start the refactor work. First I'll add FileContext to wide_events.py, then build out the files package, update all the import sites, remove the old file, and run type-checking and linting to make sure everything is clean.

Now I'm defining the FileContext TypedDict with fields for file operations like upload, delete, and update, along with metadata like file_id, filename, content type, size, and indexing status. I'll add this to WideEventFields and export it in __all__.

Next I'm creating the summaries module to handle normalizing generated file summaries into a consistent format—whether they come as a string description, a list of page summaries, or a single document summary object, I'm converting them all to a tuple of description and optional page-wise details.

Then I'm setting up the sandbox module to mirror uploaded files into the JuiceFS workspace and persist summary sidecars alongside them, with appropriate logging for file operations.

Finally I'm starting the store module to handle durable storage across Cloudinary, MongoDB, and ChromaDB for indexing and retrieval. The `index_file` function needs to accept either the raw summary result (which could be a list of DocumentSummaryModel objects or a string) since it accesses properties like `page.data.page_number` directly on those objects. For the reindex operation in the update flow, it receives a string instead, so the function signature should support both `GeneratedSummary | str`. There's also a `delete_from_index` helper that removes a file's vectors from ChromaDB with error handling, and I'm clarifying the type contract to keep the behavior consistent across both upload and update paths.

Now looking at the FileService class structure...

I see the remaining methods like delete, update, and seed_uploads. The upload method is the core operation—it validates the file, generates a unique ID and public identifier, then logs the context with file metadata including size and content type.

The upload flow runs the durable Cloudinary upload and summary generation in parallel, then processes the summary to extract a description and page-wise breakdown. I'm noticing a logging issue here: calling log.set(file=FileContext(...)) twice causes the second call to completely replace the first, losing earlier fields like file_id. I need to consolidate all the file context fields into a single log.set call after I have all the information, rather than updating it piecemeal.

My approach is to set the FileContext once, right after processing the summary, with all the identifiers and metadata at that point. For any failures that happen before that (validation, upload), the real-time log.info calls will capture them. This keeps the context stable and avoids the clobber problem. I'm also thinking through whether the search tool should use FileService for its Mongo queries or continue doing its own file collection lookups. I'm deciding to keep the refactor focused on the file_service package itself rather than refactoring the search tool's direct file queries, since that's a separate domain. The user wants file operations centralized and navigable, so I'll update the three import sites — messages.py, file.py, and stream.py — to use the new FileService class from the files package instead of importing individual functions from the old file_service module. Let me trace through those files to see the exact call patterns I need to update.

Let me read the remaining call sites in `file.py` and `stream.py` to update them precisely.

input.content:
"""Shape and render the summaries produced for user-uploaded files.

`generate_file_summary` returns one of three shapes depending on the file type
(plain string for images/text, per-page models for documents). These helpers
normalize that into the `(description, page_wise_summary)` pair stored in Mongo
and render the human-readable `<file>.summary.md` sidecar.
"""

from typing import Any

from fastapi import HTTPException

from app.models.files_models import DocumentSummaryModel
from shared.py.wide_events import log

# Raw output of `generate_file_summary`.
GeneratedSummary = str | list[DocumentSummaryModel] | DocumentSummaryModel

# Normalized page-wise summary as persisted in Mongo.
PageWiseSummary = list[dict[str, Any]] | dict[str, Any] | None


def process_summary(summary: GeneratedSummary) -> tuple[str, PageWiseSummary]:
    """Normalize a generated summary into `(description, page_wise_summary)`.

    - str            → (text, None)
    - list[pages]    → (joined page summaries, [page dicts])
    - single page    → (page summary, page dict)
    """
    if isinstance(summary, str):
        return summary, None
    if isinstance(summary, list):
        pages = [page.model_dump(mode="json") for page in summary]
        description = "".join(page.summary for page in summary)
        return description, pages
    if isinstance(summary, DocumentSummaryModel):
        return summary.summary, summary.model_dump(mode="json")

    log.error("[files] generator returned an unrecognized summary shape")
    raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="Invalid file description format")


def render_summary_markdown(
    filename: str,
    content_type: str,
    description: str | None,
    page_wise_summary: PageWiseSummary,
) -> str:
    """Render a file's stored summary into the markdown sidecar body.

    Pure projection of the Mongo-stored summary — the short description plus,
    when present, the per-page content/summaries. No recompute.
    """
    parts: list[str] = [f"# Summary: {filename}", "", f"- Type: `{content_type}`", ""]
    if description:
        parts += ["## Overview", "", description.strip(), ""]

    if isinstance(page_wise_summary, list):
        for page in page_wise_summary:
            data = page.get("data", {})
            parts += [f"## Page {data.get('page_number', '?')}", ""]
            if page.get("summary"):
                parts += [f"**Summary:** {page['summary'].strip()}", ""]
            if data.get("content"):
                parts += [str(data["content"]).strip(), ""]
    elif isinstance(page_wise_summary, dict):
        content = page_wise_summary.get("data", {}).get("content")
        if content:
            parts += ["## Content", "", str(content).strip(), ""]

    return "\n".join(parts).rstrip() + "\n"

content.text: `sandbox.py` — workspace projection:

input.content:
"""Project uploads and their summaries into the session's JuiceFS workspace.

These writes are always best-effort: Cloudinary is the durable copy and Mongo
holds the authoritative metadata, so a missing mount (native dev) or any write
failure must never fail the upload. The agent reads files from the returned
`/workspace/...` paths.
"""

import contextlib

from app.agents.workspace.paths import USER_UPLOADED_DIRNAME, safe_upload_filename
from app.services.artifact_events import publish_artifact_event, upload_event
from app.services.storage import (
    FsOps,
    JuiceFSUnavailable,
    chmod_path,
    fs_timer,
    session_root,
    write_session_file,
)
from shared.py.wide_events import log

_READ_ONLY = 0o444
_WRITABLE = 0o644


async def _overwrite_read_only(user_id: str, conversation_id: str, relative_path: str) -> None:
    """Make a prior read-only copy writable so a re-upload (last-writer-wins) can replace it."""
    prior = session_root(user_id, conversation_id) / relative_path
    with contextlib.suppress(Exception):
        if prior.exists():
            await chmod_path(prior, _WRITABLE)


async def mirror_upload(
    user_id: str,
    conversation_id: str,
    safe_filename: str,
    content: bytes,
    content_type: str,
) -> str | None:
    """Mirror an upload into the session's read-only `user-uploaded/` dir.

    Returns the `/workspace/...` path the agent can read, or None when JuiceFS
    is unavailable / the write fails.
    """
    relative_path = f"{USER_UPLOADED_DIRNAME}/{safe_filename}"
    try:
        async with fs_timer(FsOps.UPLOAD_PERSIST_SANDBOX):
            await _overwrite_read_only(user_id, conversation_id, relative_path)
            host_path, sandbox_path = await write_session_file(
                user_id=user_id,
                conversation_id=conversation_id,
                relative_path=relative_path,
                content=content,
            )
            await chmod_path(host_path, _READ_ONLY)
    except JuiceFSUnavailable as e:
        log.warning(f"[files] juicefs unavailable; not mirroring upload: {e}")
        return None
    except Exception as e:
        log.error(f"[files] failed to mirror upload to sandbox: {e!s}", exc_info=True)
        return None

    # Cross-mount host writes may not reach the sandbox watcher; publish the
    # artifact event directly so the chat UI sees the upload immediately.
    await publish_artifact_event(
        user_id,
        upload_event(
            conversation_id,
            safe_filename,
            size_bytes=len(content),
            content_type=content_type,
        ),
    )
    log.info(f"[files] mirrored upload to {sandbox_path}")
    return sandbox_path


async def write_summary_sidecar(
    user_id: str,
    conversation_id: str,
    safe_filename: str,
    summary_md: str,
) -> None:
    """Write a file's full summary to its `<file>.summary.md` sidecar.

    Best-effort, mirroring `mirror_upload`: the inline summary in the agent
    context comes from Mongo independently, so a failure here is non-fatal.
    """
    relative_path = f"{USER_UPLOADED_DIRNAME}/{safe_filename}.summary.md"
    try:
        await _overwrite_read_only(user_id, conversation_id, relative_path)
        host_path, _ = await write_session_file(
            user_id=user_id,
            conversation_id=conversation_id,
            relative_path=relative_path,
            content=summary_md.encode("utf-8"),
        )
        await chmod_path(host_path, _READ_ONLY)
        log.info(f"[files] wrote summary sidecar for {safe_filename}")
    except JuiceFSUnavailable:
        return
    except Exception as e:
        log.warning(f"[files] failed to write summary sidecar for {safe_filename}: {e!s}")


__all__ = ["mirror_upload", "write_summary_sidecar", "safe_upload_filename"]

    prior = session_root(user_id, conversation_id) / relative_path

        if prior.exists():
            await chmod_path(prior, _WRITABLE)

    user_id: str,
    conversation_id: str,
    safe_filename: str,
    content: bytes,
    content_type: str,
) -> str | None:
    """Mirror an upload into the session's read-only `user-uploaded/` dir.

    Returns the `/workspace/...` path the agent can read, or None when JuiceFS
    is unavailable / the write fails.
    """
    relative_path = f"{USER_UPLOADED_DIRNAME}/{safe_filename}"

            host_path, sandbox_path = await write_session_file(
                user_id=user_id,
                conversation_id=conversation_id,
                relative_path=relative_path,
                content=content,
            )
            await chmod_path(host_path, _READ_ONLY)
    except JuiceFSUnavailable as e:
        log.warning(f"[files] juicefs unavailable; not mirroring upload: {e}")
        return None
    except Exception as e:
        log.error(f"[files] failed to mirror upload to sandbox: {e!s}", exc_info=True)
        return None

    # Cross-mount host writes may not reach the sandbox watcher; publish the
    # artifact event directly so the chat UI sees the upload immediately.
    await publish_artifact_event(
        user_id,
        upload_event(
            conversation_id,
            safe_filename,
            size_bytes=len(content),
            content_type=content_type,
        ),
    )
    log.info(f"[files] mirrored upload to {sandbox_path}")
    return sandbox_path


async def write_summary_sidecar(
    user_id: str,
    conversation_id: str,
    safe_filename: str,
    summary_md: str,
) -> None:
    """Write a file's full summary to its `<file>.summary.md` sidecar.

    Best-effort, mirroring `mirror_upload`: the inline summary in the agent
    context comes from Mongo independently, so a failure here is non-fatal.
    """
    relative_path = f"{USER_UPLOADED_DIRNAME}/{safe_filename}.summary.md"
    try:
        await _overwrite_read_only(user_id, conversation_id, relative_path)
        host_path, _ = await write_session_file(
            user_id=user_id,
            conversation_id=conversation_id,
            relative_path=relative_path,
            content=summary_md.encode("utf-8"),
        )
        await chmod_path(host_path, _READ_ONLY)
        log.info(f"[files] wrote summary sidecar for {safe_filename}")
    except JuiceFSUnavailable:
        return
    except Exception as e:
        log.warning(f"[files] failed to write summary sidecar for {safe_filename}: {e!s}")

content.text: Now `store.py` — Cloudinary + Mongo + Chroma:

input.content:
"""Durable and indexed storage for uploaded files.

Three backends, one per concern:
- Cloudinary — the durable blob copy.
- MongoDB `files_collection` — authoritative file metadata + summary.
- ChromaDB `documents` — the vector index powering `search_uploaded_files`.

ChromaDB writes are best-effort: a failure degrades search but must never fail
the upload, so the blob + metadata are still persisted.
"""

import asyncio
import io
import uuid

import cloudinary
import cloudinary.uploader
from fastapi import HTTPException
from langchain_core.documents import Document

from app.db.chroma.chromadb import ChromaClient
from app.db.mongodb.collections import files_collection
from app.services.files.summaries import GeneratedSummary
from shared.py.wide_events import log

_CHROMA_DOCUMENTS = "documents"


async def upload_to_cloudinary(content: bytes, resource_type: str, public_id: str) -> str:
    """Upload bytes to Cloudinary and return the durable secure URL."""
    result = await asyncio.to_thread(
        cloudinary.uploader.upload,
        io.BytesIO(content),
        resource_type=resource_type,
        public_id=public_id,
        overwrite=True,
    )
    url = result.get("secure_url")
    if not url:
        log.error("[files] cloudinary upload returned no secure_url")
        raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail="Invalid response from file upload service")
    return url


def destroy_in_cloudinary(public_id: str) -> None:
    """Best-effort delete of a Cloudinary blob."""
    try:
        result = cloudinary.uploader.destroy(public_id)
        if result.get("result") != "ok":
            log.warning(f"[files] cloudinary delete did not confirm: {result}")
    except Exception as e:
        log.error(f"[files] cloudinary delete failed: {e!s}", exc_info=True)


async def insert_metadata(metadata: dict) -> None:
    """Persist a file's metadata document; raise if the insert is rejected."""
    result = await files_collection.insert_one(document=metadata)
    if not result.inserted_id:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail="Failed to store file metadata")


def _build_index_documents(
    file_id: str,
    user_id: str,
    filename: str,
    content_type: str,
    summary: GeneratedSummary,
    conversation_id: str | None,
) -> tuple[list[Document], list[str]]:
    """Turn a generated summary into ChromaDB documents + their ids.

    Multi-page documents are indexed one vector per page (each under a fresh id);
    everything else is a single vector keyed by `file_id`.
    """
    base_metadata = {"file_id": file_id, "user_id": user_id, "filename": filename, "type": content_type}
    if conversation_id:
        base_metadata["conversation_id"] = conversation_id

    if isinstance(summary, list):
        documents: list[Document] = []
        ids: list[str] = []
        for page in summary:
            documents.append(
                Document(
                    page_content=page.summary,
                    metadata={**base_metadata, "page_number": page.data.page_number},
                )
            )
            ids.append(str(uuid.uuid4()))
        return documents, ids

    page_content = summary if isinstance(summary, str) else summary.summary
    return [Document(page_content=page_content, metadata=base_metadata)], [file_id]


async def index_file(
    file_id: str,
    user_id: str,
    filename: str,
    content_type: str,
    summary: GeneratedSummary,
    conversation_id: str | None = None,
) -> None:
    """Index a file's summary into ChromaDB. Best-effort — logs and swallows failures."""
    try:
        collection = await ChromaClient.get_langchain_client(collection_name=_CHROMA_DOCUMENTS)
        documents, ids = _build_index_documents(
            file_id, user_id, filename, content_type, summary, conversation_id
        )
        await collection.aadd_documents(ids=ids, documents=documents)
        log.info(f"[files] indexed {file_id} in vector store ({len(documents)} vector(s))")
    except Exception as e:
        log.error(f"[files] failed to index {file_id} in vector store: {e!s}", exc_info=True)


async def reindex_file(
    file_id: str,
    user_id: str,
    filename: str,
    content_type: str,
    summary: GeneratedSummary,
    conversation_id: str | None = None,
) -> None:
    """Replace a file's existing vectors with a freshly indexed summary."""
    await delete_from_index(file_id)
    await index_file(file_id, user_id, filename, content_type, summary, conversation_id)


async def delete_from_index(file_id: str) -> None:
    """Best-effort removal of a file's vectors from ChromaDB."""
    try:
        collection = await ChromaClient.get_langchain_client(collection_name=_CHROMA_DOCUMENTS)
        await collection.adelete(ids=[file_id])
        log.info(f"[files] removed {file_id} from vector store")
    except Exception as e:
        log.warning(f"[files] failed to remove {file_id} from vector store: {e!s}")

input.content:
"""FileService — the single entry point for user-uploaded-file operations.

Each public method orchestrates the concern-specific helpers (`store`, `sandbox`,
`summaries`) into one readable flow. Durable storage (Cloudinary) and metadata
(Mongo) are authoritative; vector indexing and the sandbox mirror are best-effort.
"""

import asyncio
from datetime import UTC, datetime
import uuid

from fastapi import HTTPException, UploadFile
import httpx

from app.agents.workspace.paths import safe_upload_filename
from app.db.mongodb.collections import files_collection
from app.db.utils import serialize_document
from app.decorators.caching import CacheInvalidator
from app.models.message_models import FileData as MessageFileData
from app.services.files.sandbox import mirror_upload, write_summary_sidecar
from app.services.files.store import (
    delete_from_index,
    destroy_in_cloudinary,
    index_file,
    insert_metadata,
    reindex_file,
    upload_to_cloudinary,
)
from app.services.files.summaries import (
    PageWiseSummary,
    process_summary,
    render_summary_markdown,
)
from app.utils.file_utils import generate_file_summary
from app.utils.upload_validation import validate_upload
from shared.py.wide_events import FileContext, log

# Cache keys for a user's file listings, invalidated on every mutation.
_FILES_CACHE_KEY = "files:{user_id}:*"

_SEED_DOWNLOAD_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 30.0


def _page_count(page_wise_summary: PageWiseSummary) -> int:
    if isinstance(page_wise_summary, list):
        return len(page_wise_summary)
    return 1 if page_wise_summary else 0


class FileService:
    """Lifecycle of user-uploaded files: upload, summarize, index, search context, delete, update."""

    @staticmethod
    @CacheInvalidator(key_patterns=[_FILES_CACHE_KEY])
    async def upload(
        file: UploadFile,
        user_id: str,
        conversation_id: str | None = None,
        content_length: int | None = None,

"""FileService — the single entry point for user-uploaded-file operations.

Each public method orchestrates the concern-specific helpers (`store`, `sandbox`,
`summaries`) into one readable flow. Durable storage (Cloudinary) and metadata
(Mongo) are authoritative; vector indexing and the sandbox mirror are best-effort.
"""

import asyncio
from datetime import UTC, datetime
import uuid

from fastapi import HTTPException, UploadFile

    delete_from_index,
    destroy_in_cloudinary,
    index_file,
    insert_metadata,
    reindex_file,
    upload_to_cloudinary,
)
from app.services.files.summaries import (
    PageWiseSummary,
    process_summary,
    render_summary_markdown,
)

from app.utils.upload_validation import validate_upload

_FILES_CACHE_KEY = "files:{user_id}:*"

_SEED_DOWNLOAD_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 30.0

    if isinstance(page_wise_summary, list):
        return len(page_wise_summary)
    return 1 if page_wise_summary else 0

    """Lifecycle of user-uploaded files: upload, summarize, index, search context, delete, update."""

                ),
            )

                file=FileContext(
                    operation="upload",
                    file_id=file_id,
                    filename=filename,
                    content_type=content_type,
                    size_bytes=len(content),
                    conversation_id=conversation_id or "",
                    has_summary=bool(description),
                    page_count=_page_count(page_wise_summary),
                )
            )

            if conversation_id:
                await FileService._mirror_to_session(
                    user_id=user_id,
                    conversation_id=conversation_id,
                    filename=filename,
                    content=content,
                    content_type=content_type,
                    description=description,
                    page_wise_summary=page_wise_summary,
                )
            sandbox_path = (
                f"/workspace/sessions/{conversation_id}/user-uploaded/{safe_upload_filename(filename)}"
                if conversation_id
                else None

        public_id = file_data.get("public_id")
        if public_id:
            destroy_in_cloudinary(public_id)
        else:

        return {

        updated_file = await files_collection.find_one({"file_id": file_id, "user_id": user_id})
        if not updated_file:

        if description_updated:

                filename=updated_file.get("filename", ""),

        log.info(f"[files] update complete file_id={file_id}")
