"""
Cache Constants.

Centralized cache-related constants including TTL values and key prefixes.
Import these instead of defining local constants in services.
"""

# TTL values (in seconds)
ONE_YEAR_TTL = 31_536_000
SIX_MONTH_TTL = 15_552_000
ONE_DAY_TTL = 86_400
TWELVE_HOUR_TTL = 43_200
SIX_HOUR_TTL = 21_600
ONE_HOUR_TTL = 3_600
THIRTY_MINUTES_TTL = 1_800
TEN_MINUTES_TTL = 600
FIVE_MINUTES_TTL = 300


# TTL Aliases
DEFAULT_CACHE_TTL = ONE_HOUR_TTL
STATS_CACHE_TTL = THIRTY_MINUTES_TTL
CUSTOM_INT_METADATA_TTL = ONE_HOUR_TTL
SUBAGENT_CACHE_TTL = ONE_HOUR_TTL
# Subscription plan tier, cached for hot paths (rate limiting, per-request model
# routing). Eventually consistent: a plan change takes effect within the TTL.
SUBSCRIPTION_PLAN_CACHE_PREFIX = "subscription:"
SUBSCRIPTION_PLAN_CACHE_TTL = FIVE_MINUTES_TTL
OAUTH_STATE_TTL = TEN_MINUTES_TTL
OAUTH_DISCOVERY_TTL = ONE_DAY_TTL
MCP_TOOLS_CACHE_TTL = ONE_DAY_TTL
GLOBAL_TOOLS_CACHE_TTL = SIX_HOUR_TTL
USER_SKILLS_CACHE_TTL = TWELVE_HOUR_TTL
SKILLS_TEXT_CACHE_TTL = TWELVE_HOUR_TTL
INTEGRATION_INSTRUCTIONS_CACHE_TTL = ONE_DAY_TTL
COMMUNITY_CACHE_TTL = FIVE_MINUTES_TTL
FAVICON_CACHE_TTL = SIX_MONTH_TTL
SEARCH_CACHE_TTL = ONE_DAY_TTL
STREAM_TTL = FIVE_MINUTES_TTL
STATE_TOKEN_TTL = TEN_MINUTES_TTL
MOBILE_REDIRECT_TTL = FIVE_MINUTES_TTL

# Long TTLs with event-driven invalidation — short TTLs are a symptom of
# missing invalidation, not a safety net.
INTEGRATION_STATUS_CACHE_TTL = ONE_DAY_TTL
SUBAGENT_PROMPT_CACHE_TTL = ONE_DAY_TTL
PROVIDER_METADATA_CACHE_TTL = ONE_DAY_TTL
WEB_SEARCH_CACHE_TTL = TEN_MINUTES_TTL
WEBPAGE_FETCH_CACHE_TTL = THIRTY_MINUTES_TTL
WORKFLOW_GENERATION_CACHE_TTL = ONE_DAY_TTL

# Bounded in-process LRU+TTL cache for per-(integration, user) compiled
# subagent graphs. Caps RSS growth that scales with MAU × MCP integrations.
SUBAGENT_GRAPH_CACHE_MAX_SIZE = 100
SUBAGENT_GRAPH_CACHE_TTL_SECONDS = TEN_MINUTES_TTL
SUBAGENT_GRAPH_CLEANUP_INTERVAL_SECONDS = 60

# Cache key prefixes
TEAM_CACHE_PREFIX = "team"
CUSTOM_INT_METADATA_CACHE_PREFIX = "custom_int_metadata"
HANDOFF_METADATA_CACHE_PREFIX = "handoff_metadata"
# Custom-MCP display name resolved for handoff (keyed by integration id).
HANDOFF_NAME_CACHE_PREFIX = "handoff_name"
SUBAGENT_CACHE_PREFIX = "subagent_info"
OAUTH_STATE_PREFIX = "mcp_oauth_state"
OAUTH_EXCLUDED_SCOPES_PREFIX = "mcp_oauth_excluded_scopes"
# v2: discovery is now cached as the OAuthDiscovery model (model_dump) rather
# than the old ad-hoc dict; bump busts stale dict-shaped entries.
OAUTH_DISCOVERY_PREFIX = "mcp_oauth_discovery_v2"
OAUTH_STATUS_KEY = "OAUTH_STATUS"

# Every cache that derives from a user's integration set. Whenever a user's
# integrations change (add / remove / status flip), ALL of these must be busted
# together — otherwise one cache lags behind another and the views diverge (a
# stale OAUTH_STATUS hid a freshly-connected MCP from retrieve_tools while the
# tools:user:* caches already showed it). Single source so no mutation path can
# forget one. `{user_id}` is substituted by CacheInvalidator at call time.
USER_INTEGRATION_CACHE_PATTERNS = [
    "tools:user:{user_id}:*",
    "tool_namespaces:{user_id}",
    f"{OAUTH_STATUS_KEY}:{{user_id}}",
]
MCP_TOOLS_CACHE_KEY = "mcp:tools:all"
GLOBAL_TOOLS_CACHE_KEY = "tools:global"
USER_SKILLS_CACHE_KEY = "skills:user:{user_id}:agent:{agent_name}"
# v2: the listing now merges in-memory builtin skills; bump busts stale empty entries.
SKILLS_TEXT_CACHE_KEY = "skills:text:v2:{user_id}:{agent_name}"
INTEGRATION_INSTRUCTIONS_CACHE_KEY = "integration_instructions:{user_id}"
# Conversation-level artifact registry (single source of truth for a
# conversation's agent-written files). Long TTL with event-driven invalidation
# on every upsert/remove — a chat turn reads it once instead of re-scanning the
# costly JuiceFS dir.
CONV_ARTIFACTS_CACHE_PATTERN = "conv_artifacts:{user_id}:{conv_id}"
STREAM_CHANNEL_PREFIX = "stream:channel:"
STREAM_SIGNAL_PREFIX = "stream:signal:"
STREAM_PROGRESS_PREFIX = "stream:progress:"
STATE_KEY_PREFIX = "oauth_state"
# Single-use marker for login-free integration-connect links (keyed by jti).
CONNECT_LINK_USED_PREFIX = "connect_link_used"
PLATFORM_LINK_TOKEN_PREFIX = "platform_link_token"  # nosec B105
PLATFORM_LINK_TOKEN_TTL = TEN_MINUTES_TTL
# Desktop tool bridge — request ownership keys + per-request result channels.
# A request key expiring means the desktop never answered; the result endpoint
# rejects late POSTs whose key is gone.
DESKTOP_REQUEST_PREFIX = "desktop:request:"
DESKTOP_RESULT_CHANNEL_PREFIX = "desktop:result:"
# Latest desktop (Electron) release resolved from GitHub for the download page.
# Infrequent releases, so 30 min keeps the page fresh without hammering GitHub.
DESKTOP_RELEASE_CACHE_KEY = "desktop:release:latest"
DESKTOP_RELEASE_CACHE_TTL = THIRTY_MINUTES_TTL
# The ownership key's TTL is derived per-call from the awaiting tool's timeout
# plus this grace, so the key always outlives the wait (a fixed TTL could be
# outrun by a longer custom timeout, expiring mid-wait and dropping a valid
# late result). The tool deletes the key as soon as it resolves, so this TTL
# only bounds the orphaned-on-crash case.
DESKTOP_REQUEST_TTL_GRACE_SECONDS = 15
EXECUTOR_BUSY_PREFIX = "executor:busy:"
EXECUTOR_BUSY_TTL = THIRTY_MINUTES_TTL
EXECUTOR_QUEUE_PREFIX = "executor:queue:"
EXECUTOR_QUEUE_TTL = ONE_HOUR_TTL  # Tasks expire if not picked up within 1 hour
# Max time a caller waits for a detached executor to finish before draining
# whatever tool events were collected. Matches the busy lock TTL — the executor
# cannot outlive its lock, so waiting longer would be pointless.
EXECUTOR_WAIT_TIMEOUT = THIRTY_MINUTES_TTL
# ElevenLabs voice lists (account + shared library) cached for the voice picker.
ELEVENLABS_VOICES_CACHE_KEY = "voice:elevenlabs_voices"
ELEVENLABS_SHARED_VOICES_CACHE_KEY = "voice:elevenlabs_shared_voices"
# Upper bound a voice-mode stream waits for a delegated executor's narrated
# answer before sending [DONE] anyway. Real action turns resolve in a few
# seconds; on timeout the answer still reaches the user via the WebSocket push.
VOICE_EXECUTOR_RESULT_TIMEOUT_S = 90.0

toolUseResult.originalFile:
"""Durable and indexed storage for uploaded files.

Three backends, one per concern:
- Cloudinary — the durable blob copy.
- MongoDB `files_collection` — authoritative file metadata + summary.
- ChromaDB `documents` — the vector index powering `search_uploaded_files`.

ChromaDB writes are best-effort: a failure degrades search but must never fail
the upload, so the blob + metadata are still persisted.
"""

import asyncio
import io
import uuid

import cloudinary
import cloudinary.uploader
from fastapi import HTTPException
from langchain_core.documents import Document

from app.db.chroma.chromadb import ChromaClient
from app.db.mongodb.collections import files_collection
from app.services.files.summaries import GeneratedSummary
from shared.py.wide_events import log

_CHROMA_DOCUMENTS = "documents"


async def upload_to_cloudinary(content: bytes, resource_type: str, public_id: str) -> str:
    """Upload bytes to Cloudinary and return the durable secure URL."""
    result = await asyncio.to_thread(
        cloudinary.uploader.upload,
        io.BytesIO(content),
        resource_type=resource_type,
        public_id=public_id,
        overwrite=True,
    )
    url = result.get("secure_url")
    if not url:
        log.error("[files] cloudinary upload returned no secure_url")
        raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail="Invalid response from file upload service")
    return url


def destroy_in_cloudinary(public_id: str) -> None:
    """Best-effort delete of a Cloudinary blob."""
    try:
        result = cloudinary.uploader.destroy(public_id)
        if result.get("result") != "ok":
            log.warning(f"[files] cloudinary delete did not confirm: {result}")
    except Exception as e:
        log.error(f"[files] cloudinary delete failed: {e!s}", exc_info=True)


async def insert_metadata(metadata: dict) -> None:
    """Persist a file's metadata document; raise if the insert is rejected."""
    result = await files_collection.insert_one(document=metadata)
    if not result.inserted_id:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail="Failed to store file metadata")


def _build_index_documents(
    file_id: str,
    user_id: str,
    filename: str,
    content_type: str,
    summary: GeneratedSummary,
    conversation_id: str | None,
) -> tuple[list[Document], list[str]]:
    """Turn a generated summary into ChromaDB documents + their ids.

    Multi-page documents are indexed one vector per page (each under a fresh id);
    everything else is a single vector keyed by `file_id`.
    """
    base_metadata = {
        "file_id": file_id,
        "user_id": user_id,
        "filename": filename,
        "type": content_type,
    }
    if conversation_id:
        base_metadata["conversation_id"] = conversation_id

    if isinstance(summary, list):
        documents: list[Document] = []
        ids: list[str] = []
        for page in summary:
            documents.append(
                Document(
                    page_content=page.summary,
                    metadata={**base_metadata, "page_number": page.data.page_number},
                )
            )
            ids.append(str(uuid.uuid4()))
        return documents, ids

    page_content = summary if isinstance(summary, str) else summary.summary
    return [Document(page_content=page_content, metadata=base_metadata)], [file_id]


async def index_file(
    file_id: str,
    user_id: str,
    filename: str,
    content_type: str,
    summary: GeneratedSummary,
    conversation_id: str | None = None,
) -> None:
    """Index a file's summary into ChromaDB. Best-effort — logs and swallows failures."""
    try:
        collection = await ChromaClient.get_langchain_client(collection_name=_CHROMA_DOCUMENTS)
        documents, ids = _build_index_documents(
            file_id, user_id, filename, content_type, summary, conversation_id
        )
        await collection.aadd_documents(ids=ids, documents=documents)
        log.info(f"[files] indexed {file_id} in vector store ({len(documents)} vector(s))")
    except Exception as e:
        log.error(f"[files] failed to index {file_id} in vector store: {e!s}", exc_info=True)


async def reindex_file(
    file_id: str,
    user_id: str,
    filename: str,
    content_type: str,
    summary: GeneratedSummary,
    conversation_id: str | None = None,
) -> None:
    """Replace a file's existing vectors with a freshly indexed summary."""
    await delete_from_index(file_id)
    await index_file(file_id, user_id, filename, content_type, summary, conversation_id)


async def delete_from_index(file_id: str) -> None:
    """Best-effort removal of a file's vectors from ChromaDB."""
    try:

toolUseResult.originalFile:
"""Durable and indexed storage for uploaded files.

Three backends, one per concern:
- Cloudinary — the durable blob copy.
- MongoDB `files_collection` — authoritative file metadata + summary.
- ChromaDB `documents` — the vector index powering `search_uploaded_files`.

ChromaDB writes are best-effort: a failure degrades search but must never fail
the upload, so the blob + metadata are still persisted.
"""

import asyncio
import io
import uuid

import cloudinary
import cloudinary.uploader
from fastapi import HTTPException
from langchain_core.documents import Document

from app.db.chroma.chromadb import ChromaClient
from app.db.mongodb.collections import files_collection
from app.services.files.summaries import GeneratedSummary
from shared.py.wide_events import log


async def upload_to_cloudinary(content: bytes, resource_type: str, public_id: str) -> str:
    """Upload bytes to Cloudinary and return the durable secure URL."""
    result = await asyncio.to_thread(
        cloudinary.uploader.upload,
        io.BytesIO(content),
        resource_type=resource_type,
        public_id=public_id,
        overwrite=True,
    )
    url = result.get("secure_url")
    if not url:
        log.error("[files] cloudinary upload returned no secure_url")
        raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail="Invalid response from file upload service")
    return url


def destroy_in_cloudinary(public_id: str) -> None:
    """Best-effort delete of a Cloudinary blob."""
    try:
        result = cloudinary.uploader.destroy(public_id)
        if result.get("result") != "ok":
            log.warning(f"[files] cloudinary delete did not confirm: {result}")
    except Exception as e:
        log.error(f"[files] cloudinary delete failed: {e!s}", exc_info=True)


async def insert_metadata(metadata: dict) -> None:
    """Persist a file's metadata document; raise if the insert is rejected."""
    result = await files_collection.insert_one(document=metadata)
    if not result.inserted_id:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail="Failed to store file metadata")


def _build_index_documents(
    file_id: str,
    user_id: str,
    filename: str,
    content_type: str,
    summary: GeneratedSummary,
    conversation_id: str | None,
) -> tuple[list[Document], list[str]]:
    """Turn a generated summary into ChromaDB documents + their ids.

    Multi-page documents are indexed one vector per page (each under a fresh id);
    everything else is a single vector keyed by `file_id`.
    """
    base_metadata = {
        "file_id": file_id,
        "user_id": user_id,
        "filename": filename,
        "type": content_type,
    }
    if conversation_id:
        base_metadata["conversation_id"] = conversation_id

    if isinstance(summary, list):
        documents: list[Document] = []
        ids: list[str] = []
        for page in summary:
            documents.append(
                Document(
                    page_content=page.summary,
                    metadata={**base_metadata, "page_number": page.data.page_number},
                )
            )
            ids.append(str(uuid.uuid4()))
        return documents, ids

    page_content = summary if isinstance(summary, str) else summary.summary
    return [Document(page_content=page_content, metadata=base_metadata)], [file_id]


async def index_file(
    file_id: str,
    user_id: str,
    filename: str,
    content_type: str,
    summary: GeneratedSummary,
    conversation_id: str | None = None,
) -> None:
    """Index a file's summary into ChromaDB. Best-effort — logs and swallows failures."""
    try:
        collection = await ChromaClient.get_langchain_client(collection_name=_CHROMA_DOCUMENTS)
        documents, ids = _build_index_documents(
            file_id, user_id, filename, content_type, summary, conversation_id
        )
        await collection.aadd_documents(ids=ids, documents=documents)
        log.info(f"[files] indexed {file_id} in vector store ({len(documents)} vector(s))")
    except Exception as e:
        log.error(f"[files] failed to index {file_id} in vector store: {e!s}", exc_info=True)


async def reindex_file(
    file_id: str,
    user_id: str,
    filename: str,
    content_type: str,
    summary: GeneratedSummary,
    conversation_id: str | None = None,
) -> None:
    """Replace a file's existing vectors with a freshly indexed summary."""
    await delete_from_index(file_id)
    await index_file(file_id, user_id, filename, content_type, summary, conversation_id)


async def delete_from_index(file_id: str) -> None:
    """Best-effort removal of a file's vectors from ChromaDB."""
    try:
        collection = await ChromaClient.get_langchain_client(collection_name=_CHROMA_DOCUMENTS)
        await collection.adelete(ids=[file_id])
        log.info(f"[files] removed {file_id} from vector store")
    except Exception as e:
        log.warning(f"[files] failed to remove {file_id} from vector store: {e!s}")

from typing import Annotated

from langchain_core.documents import Document
from langchain_core.runnables import RunnableConfig
from langchain_core.tools import tool

from app.db.chroma.chromadb import ChromaClient
from app.db.mongodb.collections import files_collection
from app.decorators import with_doc, with_rate_limiting
from app.templates.docstrings.file_tool_docs import SEARCH_UPLOADED_FILES
from shared.py.wide_events import log


@tool
@with_rate_limiting("file_analysis")
@with_doc(SEARCH_UPLOADED_FILES)
async def search_uploaded_files(
    query: Annotated[
        str,
        "",
    ],
    file_id: Annotated[
        str | None,
        "Optional: restrict the search to one uploaded file by its ID. "
        "Omit to search across all files uploaded in this conversation.",
    ],
    config: RunnableConfig,
) -> str:
    try:
        log.set(tool={"name": "search_uploaded_files", "action": "query"})
        configurable = config.get("configurable")

        if not configurable:
            log.error("Configurable is not set in the config.")
            raise ValueError("Configurable is not set in the config.")

        conversation_id = configurable["thread_id"]

        similar_documents = await _get_similar_documents(
            query=query,
            conversation_id=conversation_id,
            file_id=file_id,
            user_id=configurable["user_id"],
        )

        log.info(f"Similar documents found: {similar_documents}")

        document_ids = list(
            set([document.metadata.get("file_id") for document, score in similar_documents])
        )

        log.info(f"Document IDs: {document_ids}")

        documents = await files_collection.find(
            filter={
                "file_id": {"$in": document_ids},
                "user_id": configurable["user_id"],
            },
        ).to_list(length=None)

        log.info(f"Documents found: {documents}")

        return _construct_content(
            documents=documents,
            similar_documents=similar_documents,
        )

    except Exception as e:
        log.error(f"Error in querying document: {e!s}")
        raise e


async def _get_similar_documents(
    query: str,
    conversation_id: str,
    user_id: str,
    file_id: str | None = None,
) -> list[tuple[Document, float]]:
    """Semantic search over files uploaded in this conversation, scored by similarity.

    Scope is resolved from MongoDB (files carrying this ``conversation_id``) and
    applied as a ``file_id`` filter on the vector search — so the tool can never
    surface a file from another conversation, regardless of ChromaDB metadata.
    """
    chroma_documents_collection = await ChromaClient.get_langchain_client(
        collection_name="documents"
    )

    if not chroma_documents_collection:
        log.error("ChromaDB client is not available.")
        return []

    conversation_files = await files_collection.find(
        {"user_id": user_id, "conversation_id": conversation_id},
        projection={"_id": 0, "file_id": 1},
    ).to_list(length=None)
    conversation_file_ids = [doc["file_id"] for doc in conversation_files if doc.get("file_id")]
    if not conversation_file_ids:
        return []

    if file_id is not None:
        if file_id not in conversation_file_ids:
            return []
        target_file_ids = [file_id]
    else:
        target_file_ids = conversation_file_ids

    filters = {
        "$and": [
            {"user_id": user_id},
            {"file_id": {"$in": target_file_ids}},
        ]
    }

    return await chroma_documents_collection.asimilarity_search_with_score(
        query=query,
        filter=filters,
        k=5,
    )


def _construct_content(documents: list, similar_documents: list[tuple[Document, float]]) -> str:
    """
    Helper function to construct a formatted response from similar documents.

    This function takes the document metadata from MongoDB and similar document sections
    from ChromaDB to build a human-readable response. It handles different document formats
    and extracts the relevant content, organizing it by document ID and page number.

    Args:
        documents: List of document metadata from MongoDB
        similar_documents: List of similar document sections from ChromaDB with similarity scores

    Returns:
        str: Formatted content string containing relevant document sections with proper
             attribution and structure for easy reading
    """
    content = ""

    for similar_document, score in similar_documents:
        document_id = similar_document.metadata["file_id"]
        document = next(
            (doc for doc in documents if str(doc["file_id"]) == str(document_id)),
            None,
        )

        if not document:
            log.error(f"Document with ID {document_id} not found.")
            continue

        document_content = document["page_wise_summary"]
        description = document["description"]

        if not document_content:
            content += f"Document ID: {document_id}\n"
            content += f"Description: {description}\n\n"
        elif isinstance(document_content, str):
            content += f"Document ID: {document_id}\n"
            content += f"Description: {document_content}\n\n"
        elif isinstance(document_content, list):
            target_page_number = similar_document.metadata["page_number"]
            for page in document_content:
                if page["data"]["page_number"] == target_page_number:
                    content += f"Document ID: {document_id}\n"
                    content += f"Page Number: {target_page_number}\n"
                    content += f"Description: {page['data']['content']}\n\n"
                    break
        elif isinstance(document_content, dict):
            content += f"Document ID: {document_id}\n"
            content += f"Description: {document_content.get('data', {}).get('content', 'Description not available!')}\n\n"
        else:
            log.error(f"Unexpected document description type: {type(document['description'])}")
            content += f"Document ID: {document_id}\n"
            content += "Description: Invalid format\n\n"

    log.info(f"Constructed content: {content}")

    return content

from typing import Annotated

from langchain_core.documents import Document
from langchain_core.runnables import RunnableConfig
from langchain_core.tools import tool

from app.db.chroma.chromadb import ChromaClient
from app.db.mongodb.collections import files_collection
from app.decorators import with_doc, with_rate_limiting
from app.templates.docstrings.file_tool_docs import SEARCH_UPLOADED_FILES
from shared.py.wide_events import log


@tool
@with_rate_limiting("file_analysis")
@with_doc(SEARCH_UPLOADED_FILES)
async def search_uploaded_files(
    query: Annotated[
        str,
        "",
    ],
    file_id: Annotated[
        str | None,
        "Optional: restrict the search to one uploaded file by its ID. "
        "Omit to search across all files uploaded in this conversation.",
    ],
    config: RunnableConfig,
) -> str:
    try:
        log.set(tool={"name": "search_uploaded_files", "action": "query"})
        configurable = config.get("configurable")

        if not configurable:
            log.error("Configurable is not set in the config.")
            raise ValueError("Configurable is not set in the config.")

        conversation_id = configurable["thread_id"]

        similar_documents = await _get_similar_documents(
            query=query,
            conversation_id=conversation_id,
            file_id=file_id,
            user_id=configurable["user_id"],
        )

        log.info(f"Similar documents found: {similar_documents}")

        document_ids = list(
            set([document.metadata.get("file_id") for document, score in similar_documents])
        )

        log.info(f"Document IDs: {document_ids}")

        documents = await files_collection.find(
            filter={
                "file_id": {"$in": document_ids},
                "user_id": configurable["user_id"],
            },
        ).to_list(length=None)

        log.info(f"Documents found: {documents}")

        return _construct_content(
            documents=documents,
            similar_documents=similar_documents,
        )

    except Exception as e:
        log.error(f"Error in querying document: {e!s}")
        raise e


async def _get_similar_documents(
    query: str,
    conversation_id: str,
    user_id: str,
    file_id: str | None = None,
) -> list[tuple[Document, float]]:
    """Semantic search over files uploaded in this conversation, scored by similarity.

    Scope is resolved from MongoDB (files carrying this ``conversation_id``) and
    applied as a ``file_id`` filter on the vector search — so the tool can never
    surface a file from another conversation, regardless of ChromaDB metadata.
    """
    chroma_documents_collection = await ChromaClient.get_langchain_client(
        collection_name=CHROMA_DOCUMENTS_COLLECTION
    )

    if not chroma_documents_collection:
        log.error("ChromaDB client is not available.")
        return []

    conversation_files = await files_collection.find(
        {"user_id": user_id, "conversation_id": conversation_id},
        projection={"_id": 0, "file_id": 1},
    ).to_list(length=None)
    conversation_file_ids = [doc["file_id"] for doc in conversation_files if doc.get("file_id")]
    if not conversation_file_ids:
        return []

    if file_id is not None:
        if file_id not in conversation_file_ids:
            return []
        target_file_ids = [file_id]
    else:
        target_file_ids = conversation_file_ids

    filters = {
        "$and": [
            {"user_id": user_id},
            {"file_id": {"$in": target_file_ids}},
        ]
    }

    return await chroma_documents_collection.asimilarity_search_with_score(
        query=query,
        filter=filters,
        k=5,
    )


def _construct_content(documents: list, similar_documents: list[tuple[Document, float]]) -> str:
    """
    Helper function to construct a formatted response from similar documents.

    This function takes the document metadata from MongoDB and similar document sections
    from ChromaDB to build a human-readable response. It handles different document formats
    and extracts the relevant content, organizing it by document ID and page number.

    Args:
        documents: List of document metadata from MongoDB
        similar_documents: List of similar document sections from ChromaDB with similarity scores

    Returns:
        str: Formatted content string containing relevant document sections with proper
             attribution and structure for easy reading
    """
    content = ""

    for similar_document, score in similar_documents:
        document_id = similar_document.metadata["file_id"]
        document = next(
            (doc for doc in documents if str(doc["file_id"]) == str(document_id)),
            None,
        )

        if not document:
            log.error(f"Document with ID {document_id} not found.")
            continue

        document_content = document["page_wise_summary"]
        description = document["description"]

        if not document_content:
            content += f"Document ID: {document_id}\n"
            content += f"Description: {description}\n\n"
        elif isinstance(document_content, str):
            content += f"Document ID: {document_id}\n"
            content += f"Description: {document_content}\n\n"
        elif isinstance(document_content, list):
            target_page_number = similar_document.metadata["page_number"]
            for page in document_content:
                if page["data"]["page_number"] == target_page_number:
                    content += f"Document ID: {document_id}\n"
                    content += f"Page Number: {target_page_number}\n"
                    content += f"Description: {page['data']['content']}\n\n"
                    break
        elif isinstance(document_content, dict):
            content += f"Document ID: {document_id}\n"
            content += f"Description: {document_content.get('data', {}).get('content', 'Description not available!')}\n\n"
        else:
            log.error(f"Unexpected document description type: {type(document['description'])}")
            content += f"Document ID: {document_id}\n"
            content += "Description: Invalid format\n\n"

    log.info(f"Constructed content: {content}")

    return content

toolUseResult.originalFile:
"""Conversation-level artifact registry — the single source of truth for a
conversation's agent-written files.

Each artifact is stored once on ``conversations_collection.artifacts[]``, keyed
by ``path`` (upsert-by-path), so the same file never lands in two places and an
edit updates the one record everywhere. Reads are Redis-cached so a chat turn
reads the registry once instead of re-scanning the costly JuiceFS dir; every
write busts that cache. ``$currentDate {updatedAt}`` on each write is what makes
the frontend's batch-sync staleness check refetch the conversation afterwards.
"""

from datetime import UTC, datetime
from typing import Any

from app.constants.cache import CONV_ARTIFACTS_CACHE_PATTERN, ONE_DAY_TTL
from app.db.mongodb.collections import conversations_collection
from app.decorators.caching import Cacheable, CacheInvalidator

# Stored on each registry element alongside ``updated_at`` (and ``body`` when the
# write carries an inline preview).
_ELEMENT_FIELDS = ("path", "size_bytes", "mtime", "content_type")


@CacheInvalidator(key_patterns=[CONV_ARTIFACTS_CACHE_PATTERN])
async def upsert_conversation_artifact(user_id: str, conv_id: str, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
    """Upsert one artifact onto the conversation registry, keyed by ``path``.

    ``body`` is written only when the payload carries one, so a body-less watcher
    re-emit never wipes an inline preview saved by an earlier tool-sourced write.
    """
    now_iso = datetime.now(UTC).isoformat()
    path = payload["path"]

    set_fields: dict[str, Any] = {
        "artifacts.$.size_bytes": payload.get("size_bytes"),
        "artifacts.$.mtime": payload.get("mtime"),
        "artifacts.$.content_type": payload.get("content_type"),
        "artifacts.$.updated_at": now_iso,
    }
    if payload.get("body") is not None:
        set_fields["artifacts.$.body"] = payload["body"]

    result = await conversations_collection.update_one(
        {"user_id": user_id, "conversation_id": conv_id, "artifacts.path": path},
        {"$set": set_fields, "$currentDate": {"updatedAt": True}},
    )
    if result.matched_count:
        return

    # No existing element — push a new one. The ``$ne`` guard makes concurrent
    # inserts idempotent: only one of two racing pushes can match.
    element: dict[str, Any] = {key: payload.get(key) for key in _ELEMENT_FIELDS}
    element["updated_at"] = now_iso
    if payload.get("body") is not None:
        element["body"] = payload["body"]
    await conversations_collection.update_one(
        {"user_id": user_id, "conversation_id": conv_id, "artifacts.path": {"$ne": path}},
        {"$push": {"artifacts": element}, "$currentDate": {"updatedAt": True}},
    )


@CacheInvalidator(key_patterns=[CONV_ARTIFACTS_CACHE_PATTERN])
async def remove_conversation_artifact(user_id: str, conv_id: str, path: str) -> None:
    """Remove an artifact from the conversation registry by ``path``."""
    await conversations_collection.update_one(
        {"user_id": user_id, "conversation_id": conv_id},
        {"$pull": {"artifacts": {"path": path}}, "$currentDate": {"updatedAt": True}},
    )


@Cacheable(key_pattern=CONV_ARTIFACTS_CACHE_PATTERN, ttl=ONE_DAY_TTL, namespace="api")
async def get_conversation_artifacts(user_id: str, conv_id: str) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
    """Return the conversation's artifact registry (Redis-cached)."""
    doc = await conversations_collection.find_one(
        {"user_id": user_id, "conversation_id": conv_id},
        {"artifacts": 1, "_id": 0},
    )
    if not doc:
        return []
    return doc.get("artifacts") or []

input.old_string:
_LOG_PREFIX = "[artifacts]"

# The bot message row is written by ``_persist_turn`` *after* the forwarder
# starts, so an artifact created early in the turn can arrive before the row
# exists (``matched_count == 0``). Retry briefly to bridge that window — the row
# lands within a few hundred ms of the forwarder starting.
_PERSIST_MAX_ATTEMPTS = 5
_PERSIST_RETRY_BASE_DELAY = 0.1

# JuiceFS default block size: reading in block-sized chunks pulls each block into
# the local cache with no wasted partial fetches.
_WARM_CHUNK_BYTES = 4 * 1024 * 1024

# Cap concurrent cache-warm reads so an artifact burst in one turn can't saturate
# the shared default thread pool (``asyncio.to_thread``) and starve other IO.
_WARM_MAX_CONCURRENCY = 4
_warm_semaphore = asyncio.Semaphore(_WARM_MAX_CONCURRENCY)

content.subject: Add description field to FileData + Mongo fetch service
content.description: Add description: str | None = None to FileData in message_models.py. Add async get_file_descriptions(file_ids, user_id) -> dict[str,str] service in file_service.py (batched find, projection file_id+description).

content.subject: Enrich + inject inline summary into comms context
content.description: In construct_langchain_messages, batched-enrich FileData.description from Mongo before format_files_list. Update format_files_list to emit path + summary-file path + truncated description with read-more pointer + single-contract guidance. Add UPLOADED_FILE_INLINE_SUMMARY_MAX_CHARS to constants/chat.py.

content.subject: Write full summary file next to upload
content.description: Add markdown renderer + _persist_summary_to_sandbox in file_service.py writing user-uploaded/<file>.summary.md via write_session_file (best-effort). Call from upload_file_service and _seed_one_upload.
content.activeForm: Writing summary file next to upload

content.subject: Rename + conversation-scope the search tool
content.description: Rename query_file -> search_uploaded_files (file_tools.py, registry.py, tool_labels.py, workflow_prompts.py). Rewrite QUERY_FILE docstring. Scope _get_similar_documents to the conversation's file_ids resolved from Mongo.

content.subject: Stamp conversation_id at seed time + endpoint description
content.description: In _seed_one_upload, update Mongo files_collection conversation_id (forward-only). Return real description from upload endpoint (file.py) for UI preview.

content.subject: Type-check + lint
content.description: Run nx type-check api and nx lint api; fix any issues.

content.subject: Add FileContext to wide_events
content.description: Add FileContext TypedDict + register in WideEventFields + __all__ in libs/shared/py/wide_events.py.

content.subject: Create files/ package (summaries, sandbox, store)
content.description: Split file_service.py into app/services/files/: summaries.py (process_summary, render_summary_markdown), sandbox.py (mirror_upload, write_summary_sidecar), store.py (cloudinary/mongo/chroma helpers). Greppable [files] logging.

content.subject: Create FileService facade + __init__
content.description: FileService class in service.py with upload/get_descriptions/delete/update/seed_uploads static methods orchestrating the helpers, with FileContext + step logging. __init__ exports FileService.

content.subject: Update import sites + delete old file_service
content.description: Update messages.py, file.py, stream.py to use FileService. Delete app/services/file_service.py.

content.subject: Type-check + lint (refactor)
content.description: nx type-check api && nx lint api after the refactor; fix issues.

content.subject: files/store.py: extract documents collection const
content.description: Move _CHROMA_DOCUMENTS to constants/files.py CHROMA_DOCUMENTS_COLLECTION; dedupe the same literal in file_tools.py.

content.subject: files/service.py: extract cache key + seed timeout
content.description: Move _FILES_CACHE_KEY to cache.py FILES_CACHE_PATTERN; _SEED_DOWNLOAD_TIMEOUT_SECONDS to constants/files.py.

content.subject: artifacts_registry.py: extract element fields
content.description: Move _ELEMENT_FIELDS to constants/artifacts.py ARTIFACT_ELEMENT_FIELDS.

content.subject: artifact_forwarder.py: extract constants + entry builders
content.description: Move config constants to constants/artifacts.py; move _full_entry/_ref_entry to utils/artifact_utils.py.

content.subject: Type-check + lint (constants cleanup)
content.description: nx type-check api && nx lint api after moving constants.

file.content:
return None
    if payload.get("session_id") != conversation_id:
        return None
    return payload


def _full_entry(payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
    """A live-stream ``artifact_data`` chunk carrying the file's full data."""
    return {
        "tool_name": "artifact_data",
        "data": payload,
        "timestamp": datetime.now(UTC).isoformat(),
        "tool_category": "artifact",
    }


def _ref_entry(conversation_id: str, path: str, event: str | None) -> dict[str, Any]:
    """A lightweight ``artifact_data`` reference for a persisted message.

    Full artifact data lives in the conversation registry; the message stores
    only the fields the frontend needs to resolve it back to a card.
    """
    return {
        "tool_name": "artifact_data",
        "data": {"session_id": conversation_id, "path": path, "event": event},
        "timestamp": datetime.now(UTC).isoformat(),
        "tool_category": "artifact",
    }


def _warm_artifact_blocks(host_path: Path) -> None:
    """Read a file through the FUSE mount to pull its blocks into the JuiceFS
    local cache. Constant memory: chunks are read and discarded."""
    with host_path.open("rb") as fh:
        # Drain the file in fixed-size chunks, discarding each: the read is the
        # side effect (it pulls blocks into the cache), the bytes aren't kept.
        deque(iter(lambda: fh.read(ARTIFACT_WARM_CHUNK_BYTES), b""), maxlen=0)

content.subject: Add description field to FileData + Mongo fetch service
content.description: Add description: str | None = None to FileData in message_models.py. Add async get_file_descriptions(file_ids, user_id) -> dict[str,str] service in file_service.py (batched find, projection file_id+description).

content.subject: Enrich + inject inline summary into comms context
content.description: In construct_langchain_messages, batched-enrich FileData.description from Mongo before format_files_list. Update format_files_list to emit path + summary-file path + truncated description with read-more pointer + single-contract guidance. Add UPLOADED_FILE_INLINE_SUMMARY_MAX_CHARS to constants/chat.py.

content.subject: Write full summary file next to upload
content.description: Add markdown renderer + _persist_summary_to_sandbox in file_service.py writing user-uploaded/<file>.summary.md via write_session_file (best-effort). Call from upload_file_service and _seed_one_upload.
content.activeForm: Writing summary file next to upload

content.subject: Rename + conversation-scope the search tool
content.description: Rename query_file -> search_uploaded_files (file_tools.py, registry.py, tool_labels.py, workflow_prompts.py). Rewrite QUERY_FILE docstring. Scope _get_similar_documents to the conversation's file_ids resolved from Mongo.

content.subject: Stamp conversation_id at seed time + endpoint description
content.description: In _seed_one_upload, update Mongo files_collection conversation_id (forward-only). Return real description from upload endpoint (file.py) for UI preview.

content.subject: Type-check + lint
content.description: Run nx type-check api and nx lint api; fix any issues.

content.subject: Add FileContext to wide_events
content.description: Add FileContext TypedDict + register in WideEventFields + __all__ in libs/shared/py/wide_events.py.

content.subject: Create files/ package (summaries, sandbox, store)
content.description: Split file_service.py into app/services/files/: summaries.py (process_summary, render_summary_markdown), sandbox.py (mirror_upload, write_summary_sidecar), store.py (cloudinary/mongo/chroma helpers). Greppable [files] logging.

content.subject: Create FileService facade + __init__
content.description: FileService class in service.py with upload/get_descriptions/delete/update/seed_uploads static methods orchestrating the helpers, with FileContext + step logging. __init__ exports FileService.

content.subject: Update import sites + delete old file_service
content.description: Update messages.py, file.py, stream.py to use FileService. Delete app/services/file_service.py.

content.subject: Type-check + lint (refactor)
content.description: nx type-check api && nx lint api after the refactor; fix issues.

content.subject: files/store.py: extract documents collection const
content.description: Move _CHROMA_DOCUMENTS to constants/files.py CHROMA_DOCUMENTS_COLLECTION; dedupe the same literal in file_tools.py.

content.subject: files/service.py: extract cache key + seed timeout
content.description: Move _FILES_CACHE_KEY to cache.py FILES_CACHE_PATTERN; _SEED_DOWNLOAD_TIMEOUT_SECONDS to constants/files.py.

content.subject: artifacts_registry.py: extract element fields
content.description: Move _ELEMENT_FIELDS to constants/artifacts.py ARTIFACT_ELEMENT_FIELDS.

content.subject: artifact_forwarder.py: extract constants + entry builders
content.description: Move config constants to constants/artifacts.py; move _full_entry/_ref_entry to utils/artifact_utils.py.

content.subject: Type-check + lint (constants cleanup)
content.description: nx type-check api && nx lint api after moving constants.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
"""Durable and indexed storage for uploaded files.

Three backends, one per concern:
- Cloudinary — the durable blob copy.
- MongoDB `files_collection` — authoritative file metadata + summary.
- ChromaDB `documents` — the vector index powering `search_uploaded_files`.

ChromaDB writes are best-effort: a failure degrades search but must never fail
the upload, so the blob + metadata are still persisted.
"""

import asyncio
import io
import uuid

import cloudinary
import cloudinary.uploader
from fastapi import HTTPException
from langchain_core.documents import Document

from app.db.chroma.chromadb import ChromaClient
from app.db.mongodb.collections import files_collection
from app.services.files.summaries import GeneratedSummary
from shared.py.wide_events import log


async def upload_to_cloudinary(content: bytes, resource_type: str, public_id: str) -> str:
    """Upload bytes to Cloudinary and return the durable secure URL."""
    result = await asyncio.to_thread(
        cloudinary.uploader.upload,
        io.BytesIO(content),
        resource_type=resource_type,
        public_id=public_id,
        overwrite=True,
    )
    url = result.get("secure_url")
    if not url:
        log.error("[files] cloudinary upload returned no secure_url")
        raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail="Invalid response from file upload service")
    return url


def destroy_in_cloudinary(public_id: str) -> None:
    """Best-effort delete of a Cloudinary blob."""
    try:
        result = cloudinary.uploader.destroy(public_id)
        if result.get("result") != "ok":
            log.warning(f"[files] cloudinary delete did not confirm: {result}")
    except Exception as e:
        log.error(f"[files] cloudinary delete failed: {e!s}", exc_info=True)


async def insert_metadata(metadata: dict) -> None:
    """Persist a file's metadata document; raise if the insert is rejected."""
    result = await files_collection.insert_one(document=metadata)
    if not result.inserted_id:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail="Failed to store file metadata")


def _build_index_documents(
    file_id: str,
    user_id: str,
    filename: str,
    content_type: str,
    summary: GeneratedSummary,
    conversation_id: str | None,
) -> tuple[list[Document], list[str]]:
    """Turn a generated summary into ChromaDB documents + their ids.

    Multi-page documents are indexed one vector per page (each under a fresh id);
    everything else is a single vector keyed by `file_id`.
    """
    base_metadata = {
        "file_id": file_id,
        "user_id": user_id,
        "filename": filename,
        "type": content_type,
    }
    if conversation_id:
        base_metadata["conversation_id"] = conversation_id

    if isinstance(summary, list):
        documents: list[Document] = []
        ids: list[str] = []
        for page in summary:
            documents.append(
                Document(
                    page_content=page.summary,
                    metadata={**base_metadata, "page_number": page.data.page_number},
                )
            )
            ids.append(str(uuid.uuid4()))
        return documents, ids

    page_content = summary if isinstance(summary, str) else summary.summary
    return [Document(page_content=page_content, metadata=base_metadata)], [file_id]


async def index_file(
    file_id: str,
    user_id: str,
    filename: str,
    content_type: str,
    summary: GeneratedSummary,
    conversation_id: str | None = None,
) -> None:
    """Index a file's summary into ChromaDB. Best-effort — logs and swallows failures."""
    try:
        collection = await ChromaClient.get_langchain_client(
            collection_name=CHROMA_DOCUMENTS_COLLECTION
        )
        documents, ids = _build_index_documents(
            file_id, user_id, filename, content_type, summary, conversation_id
        )
        await collection.aadd_documents(ids=ids, documents=documents)
        log.info(f"[files] indexed {file_id} in vector store ({len(documents)} vector(s))")
    except Exception as e:
        log.error(f"[files] failed to index {file_id} in vector store: {e!s}", exc_info=True)


async def reindex_file(
    file_id: str,
    user_id: str,
    filename: str,
    content_type: str,
    summary: GeneratedSummary,
    conversation_id: str | None = None,
) -> None:
    """Replace a file's existing vectors with a freshly indexed summary."""
    await delete_from_index(file_id)
    await index_file(file_id, user_id, filename, content_type, summary, conversation_id)


async def delete_from_index(file_id: str) -> None:
    """Best-effort removal of a file's vectors from ChromaDB."""
    try:
        collection = await ChromaClient.get_langchain_client(
            collection_name=CHROMA_DOCUMENTS_COLLECTION
        )
        await collection.adelete(ids=[file_id])
        log.info(f"[files] removed {file_id} from vector store")
    except Exception as e:
        log.warning(f"[files] failed to remove {file_id} from vector store: {e!s}")

toolUseResult.originalFile:
"""Conversation-level artifact registry — the single source of truth for a
conversation's agent-written files.

Each artifact is stored once on ``conversations_collection.artifacts[]``, keyed
by ``path`` (upsert-by-path), so the same file never lands in two places and an
edit updates the one record everywhere. Reads are Redis-cached so a chat turn
reads the registry once instead of re-scanning the costly JuiceFS dir; every
write busts that cache. ``$currentDate {updatedAt}`` on each write is what makes
the frontend's batch-sync staleness check refetch the conversation afterwards.
"""

from datetime import UTC, datetime
from typing import Any

from app.constants.cache import CONV_ARTIFACTS_CACHE_PATTERN, ONE_DAY_TTL
from app.db.mongodb.collections import conversations_collection
from app.decorators.caching import Cacheable, CacheInvalidator


@CacheInvalidator(key_patterns=[CONV_ARTIFACTS_CACHE_PATTERN])
async def upsert_conversation_artifact(user_id: str, conv_id: str, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
    """Upsert one artifact onto the conversation registry, keyed by ``path``.

    ``body`` is written only when the payload carries one, so a body-less watcher
    re-emit never wipes an inline preview saved by an earlier tool-sourced write.
    """
    now_iso = datetime.now(UTC).isoformat()
    path = payload["path"]

    set_fields: dict[str, Any] = {
        "artifacts.$.size_bytes": payload.get("size_bytes"),
        "artifacts.$.mtime": payload.get("mtime"),
        "artifacts.$.content_type": payload.get("content_type"),
        "artifacts.$.updated_at": now_iso,
    }
    if payload.get("body") is not None:
        set_fields["artifacts.$.body"] = payload["body"]

    result = await conversations_collection.update_one(
        {"user_id": user_id, "conversation_id": conv_id, "artifacts.path": path},
        {"$set": set_fields, "$currentDate": {"updatedAt": True}},
    )
    if result.matched_count:
        return

    # No existing element — push a new one. The ``$ne`` guard makes concurrent
    # inserts idempotent: only one of two racing pushes can match.
    element: dict[str, Any] = {key: payload.get(key) for key in ARTIFACT_ELEMENT_FIELDS}
    element["updated_at"] = now_iso
    if payload.get("body") is not None:
        element["body"] = payload["body"]
    await conversations_collection.update_one(
        {"user_id": user_id, "conversation_id": conv_id, "artifacts.path": {"$ne": path}},
        {"$push": {"artifacts": element}, "$currentDate": {"updatedAt": True}},
    )


@CacheInvalidator(key_patterns=[CONV_ARTIFACTS_CACHE_PATTERN])
async def remove_conversation_artifact(user_id: str, conv_id: str, path: str) -> None:
    """Remove an artifact from the conversation registry by ``path``."""
    await conversations_collection.update_one(
        {"user_id": user_id, "conversation_id": conv_id},
        {"$pull": {"artifacts": {"path": path}}, "$currentDate": {"updatedAt": True}},
    )


@Cacheable(key_pattern=CONV_ARTIFACTS_CACHE_PATTERN, ttl=ONE_DAY_TTL, namespace="api")
async def get_conversation_artifacts(user_id: str, conv_id: str) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
    """Return the conversation's artifact registry (Redis-cached)."""
    doc = await conversations_collection.find_one(
        {"user_id": user_id, "conversation_id": conv_id},
        {"artifacts": 1, "_id": 0},
    )
    if not doc:
        return []
    return doc.get("artifacts") or []

content.text:
All five review comments resolved. Type-check (api) and lint (api) pass; every touched module imports cleanly.

## What each comment became

| Comment | File:line | Resolution |
|---|---|---|
| "move to constants file" | `files/store.py:26` | `_CHROMA_DOCUMENTS` → new **`constants/files.py`** `CHROMA_DOCUMENTS_COLLECTION` — also deduped the same `"documents"` literal in `file_tools.py` |
| "constants move" | `files/service.py:39` | `_FILES_CACHE_KEY` → **`constants/cache.py`** `FILES_CACHE_PATTERN` (next to the other cache patterns); `_SEED_DOWNLOAD_TIMEOUT_SECONDS` → **`constants/files.py`** `FILE_SEED_DOWNLOAD_TIMEOUT_SECONDS` |
| "move to constants file" | `artifacts_registry.py:21` | `_ELEMENT_FIELDS` → new **`constants/artifacts.py`** `ARTIFACT_ELEMENT_FIELDS` |
| "move constants to constants file" | `artifact_forwarder.py:38` | log prefix + persist/warm tuning constants → **`constants/artifacts.py`** (`ARTIFACT_LOG_PREFIX`, `ARTIFACT_PERSIST_*`, `ARTIFACT_WARM_*`) |
| "move to utils file" | `artifact_forwarder.py:304` | `_full_entry`/`_ref_entry` → new **`utils/artifact_utils.py`** as `build_artifact_full_entry`/`build_artifact_ref_entry` |

New files: `constants/files.py`, `constants/artifacts.py`, `utils/artifact_utils.py`. Runtime state (`_warm_semaphore`, task-ref sets) correctly stayed in the forwarder — those aren't constants.

**Note:** comments 1, 2, 3 were on `artifacts_registry.py` / `artifact_forwarder.py` — a separate, polished subsystem on this branch that I didn't author. I made the requested mechanical extractions there without altering behavior, but flag it in case that file is another agent's in-flight work you'd want to keep my diff out of.

Heads-up on a recurring snag: the ruff autofix hook keeps removing a freshly-added import when I add it a step before its first use (it looks momentarily unused). I caught and re-added each via type-check both times, but it's worth knowing the pattern.

Everything is still uncommitted across all the work in this session (the file-context feature, the `FileService` refactor, and now these constant extractions). Want me to commit, and if so, as one commit or split logically?

content.thinking:
The user wants to refactor the file upload flow we've built to be more readable and maintainable at a glance. We've already split things into a files package with a FileService facade and separate modules for summaries, sandbox, and storage, but they want to push the clarity further so the entire end-to-end process is immediately understandable.

I'm thinking the key is to make the `upload` method read like a clear sequence of steps rather than dense inline logic. I could extract each major phase—validation, parallel blob storage and summarization, mirroring to sandbox, persisting metadata and indexing—into clearly-named private methods so the orchestration method itself becomes a readable roadmap of what happens.

content.text: Let me re-read the current state of the flow files before deciding what actually improves "at-a-glance" readability — I don't want to churn for its own sake.

content.content:
1	"""FileService — the single entry point for user-uploaded-file operations.
2	
3	Each public method orchestrates the concern-specific helpers (`store`, `sandbox`,
4	`summaries`) into one readable flow. Durable storage (Cloudinary) and metadata
5	(Mongo) are authoritative; vector indexing and the sandbox mirror are best-effort.
6	"""
7	
8	import asyncio
9	from datetime import UTC, datetime
10	import uuid
11	
12	from fastapi import HTTPException, UploadFile
13	import httpx
14	
15	from app.agents.workspace.paths import safe_upload_filename
16	from app.constants.cache import FILES_CACHE_PATTERN
17	from app.constants.files import FILE_SEED_DOWNLOAD_TIMEOUT_SECONDS
18	from app.db.mongodb.collections import files_collection
19	from app.db.utils import serialize_document
20	from app.decorators.caching import CacheInvalidator
21	from app.models.message_models import FileData as MessageFileData
22	from app.services.files.sandbox import mirror_upload, write_summary_sidecar
23	from app.services.files.store import (
24	    delete_from_index,
25	    destroy_in_cloudinary,
26	    index_file,
27	    insert_metadata,
28	    reindex_file,
29	    upload_to_cloudinary,
30	)
31	from app.services.files.summaries import (
32	    PageWiseSummary,
33	    process_summary,
34	    render_summary_markdown,
35	)
36	from app.utils.file_utils import generate_file_summary
37	from app.utils.upload_validation import validate_upload
38	from shared.py.wide_events import FileContext, log
39	
40	
41	def _page_count(page_wise_summary: PageWiseSummary) -> int:
42	    if isinstance(page_wise_summary, list):
43	        return len(page_wise_summary)
44	    return 1 if page_wise_summary else 0
45	
46	
47	class FileService:
48	    """Lifecycle of user-uploaded files: upload, summarize, index, search context, delete, update."""
49	
50	    @staticmethod
51	    @CacheInvalidator(key_patterns=[FILES_CACHE_PATTERN])
52	    async def upload(
53	        file: UploadFile,
54	        user_id: str,
55	        conversation_id: str | None = None,
56	        content_length: int | None = None,
57	    ) -> dict:
58	        """Validate, store, summarize, and mirror an upload into the session.
59	
60	        Cloudinary (blob) + Mongo (metadata) always persist. The summary, the
61	        vector index, and the sandbox copy + `.summary.md` sidecar are layered on
62	        top; the latter two need JuiceFS and degrade gracefully without it.
63	        """
64	        content, content_type, resource_type = await validate_upload(
65	            file=file, content_length=content_length
66	        )
67	        file_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
68	        # validate_upload() guarantees a filename; narrow the type without an assert (bandit B101).
69	        filename = file.filename or ""
70	        public_id = f"file_{file_id}_{filename.replace(' ', '_')}"
71	        log.info(f"[files] upload start file_id={file_id} name={filename!r} type={content_type}")
72	
73	        try:
74	            file_url, generated_summary = await asyncio.gather(
75	                upload_to_cloudinary(content, resource_type, public_id),
76	                generate_file_summary(
77	                    file_content=content, content_type=content_type, filename=filename
78	                ),
79	            )
80	            description, page_wise_summary = process_summary(generated_summary)
81	            log.set(
82	                file=FileContext(
83	                    operation="upload",
84	                    file_id=file_id,
85	                    filename=filename,
86	                    content_type=content_type,
87	                    size_bytes=len(content),
88	                    conversation_id=conversation_id or "",
89	                    has_summary=bool(description),
90	                    page_count=_page_count(page_wise_summary),
91	                )
92	            )
93	
94	            sandbox_path: str | None = None
95	            if conversation_id:
96	                sandbox_path = await FileService._mirror_to_session(
97	                    user_id=user_id,
98	                    conversation_id=conversation_id,
99	                    filename=filename,
100	                    content=content,
101	                    content_type=content_type,
102	                    description=description,
103	                    page_wise_summary=page_wise_summary,
104	                )
105	
106	            now = datetime.now(UTC)
107	            metadata = {
108	                "file_id": file_id,
109	                "filename": filename,
110	                "type": content_type,
111	                "size": len(content),
112	                "url": file_url,
113	                "public_id": public_id,
114	                "user_id": user_id,
115	                "description": description,
116	                "page_wise_summary": page_wise_summary,
117	                "sandbox_path": sandbox_path,
118	                "created_at": now,
119	                "updated_at": now,
120	            }
121	            if conversation_id:
122	                metadata["conversation_id"] = conversation_id
123	
124	            await asyncio.gather(
125	                insert_metadata(metadata),
126	                index_file(
127	                    file_id=file_id,
128	                    user_id=user_id,
129	                    filename=filename,
130	                    content_type=content_type,
131	                    summary=generated_summary,
132	                    conversation_id=conversation_id,
133	                ),
134	            )
135	            log.info(f"[files] upload complete file_id={file_id}")
136	
137	            return {
138	                "file_id": file_id,
139	                "url": file_url,
140	                "filename": filename,
141	                "description": description,
142	                "type": content_type,
143	                "sandbox_path": sandbox_path,
144	            }
145	        except HTTPException:
146	            raise
147	        except Exception as e:
148	            log.error(f"[files] upload failed file_id={file_id}: {e!s}", exc_info=True)
149	            raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail=f"Failed to upload file: {e!s}")
150	
151	    @staticmethod
152	    async def get_descriptions(file_ids: list[str], user_id: str) -> dict[str, str]:
153	        """Return `{file_id: description}` for the user's files, in one batched query.
154	
155	        Authoritative source for the agent's file context — never trust the
156	        client request for this. Files without a stored summary are omitted.
157	        """
158	        if not file_ids:
159	            return {}
160	
161	        docs = await files_collection.find(
162	            {"file_id": {"$in": file_ids}, "user_id": user_id},
163	            projection={"_id": 0, "file_id": 1, "description": 1},
164	        ).to_list(length=None)
165	        return {
166	            doc["file_id"]: doc["description"]
167	            for doc in docs
168	            if doc.get("file_id") and doc.get("description")
169	        }
170	
171	    @staticmethod
172	    @CacheInvalidator(key_patterns=[FILES_CACHE_PATTERN])
173	    async def delete(file_id: str, user_id: str | None) -> dict:
174	        """Delete a file from Mongo, Cloudinary, and the vector index."""
175	        log.info(f"[files] delete start file_id={file_id}")
176	        if user_id is None:
177	            raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="User ID is required")
178	        log.set(file=FileContext(operation="delete", file_id=file_id))
179	
180	        file_data = await files_collection.find_one({"file_id": file_id, "user_id": user_id})
181	        if not file_data:
182	            log.warning(f"[files] delete: file_id={file_id} not found for user")
183	            raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="File not found")
184	
185	        result = await files_collection.delete_one({"file_id": file_id, "user_id": user_id})
186	        if result.deleted_count == 0:
187	            raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="File not found")
188	
189	        public_id = file_data.get("public_id")
190	        if public_id:
191	            destroy_in_cloudinary(public_id)
192	        else:
193	            log.warning(f"[files] delete: file_id={file_id} has no public_id; skipping blob delete")
194	
195	        await delete_from_index(file_id)
196	        log.info(f"[files] delete complete file_id={file_id}")
197	
198	        return {
199	            "message": "File deleted successfully",
200	            "file_id": file_id,
201	            "filename": file_data.get("filename", "Unknown"),
202	        }
203	
204	    @staticmethod
205	    @CacheInvalidator(key_patterns=[FILES_CACHE_PATTERN])
206	    async def update(
207	        file_id: str,
208	        user_id: str,
209	        update_data: dict,
210	        file_content: bytes | None = None,
211	        conversation_id: str | None = None,
212	    ) -> dict:
213	        """Update file metadata, regenerating the summary + vector index when new content is given."""
214	        log.info(f"[files] update start file_id={file_id}")
215	        log.set(file=FileContext(operation="update", file_id=file_id))
216	
217	        file_data = await files_collection.find_one({"file_id": file_id, "user_id": user_id})
218	        if not file_data:
219	            raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="File not found")
220	
221	        conversation_id = conversation_id or file_data.get("conversation_id")
222	        update_data["updated_at"] = datetime.now(UTC)
223	
224	        if file_content:
225	            try:
226	                content_type = update_data.get("type") or file_data.get("type")
